# ['Hotel California, Motel Minnesota' by Steve Abramowicz ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6729589&post_id=186610473&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NjEwNDczLCJpYXQiOjE3NzAwNDQzNjEsImV4cCI6MTc3MjYzNjM2MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTY3Mjk1ODkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.r7KHNQz1MhG7XHOKNXH764LL7vtEzwcuTE3_qh6GUYI)
### Same political song, different key: check in, cash out, and somehow we're the ones paying the minibar bill.
| | [Heartland Journal ®](https://substack.com/@heartlandjournal)
---
| Feb 2
---
| [](https://substack.com/@heartlandjournal)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6729589&post_id=186610473&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NjEwNDczLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3MDA0NDM2MSwiZXhwIjoxNzcyNjM2MzYxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjcyOTU4OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.bJyAFd6WR_wVz_jGY4C49YYTZ3VxYHGuDjxztGYGdKs&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6729589&post_id=186610473&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NjEwNDczLCJpYXQiOjE3NzAwNDQzNjEsImV4cCI6MTc3MjYzNjM2MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTY3Mjk1ODkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.r7KHNQz1MhG7XHOKNXH764LL7vtEzwcuTE3_qh6GUYI&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6729589&post_id=186610473&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NjEwNDczLCJpYXQiOjE3NzAwNDQzNjEsImV4cCI6MTc3MjYzNjM2MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTY3Mjk1ODkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.r7KHNQz1MhG7XHOKNXH764LL7vtEzwcuTE3_qh6GUYI)
---
| | [](https://substack..LLostC7MWSYENqIvDYWyKje6LagZau9Rcg2ATTuU8to?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/heartlandjournal/p/hotel-california-motel-minnesota?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/1cbc504e-2b7f-44fc-ac70-3f4f1b30fea2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
California and Minnesota have faced significant criticism for government waste, fraud, and mismanagement, with several high-profile cases highlighted in recent news and audits.

During the COVID-19 pandemic, the California Employment Development Department (EDD) experienced massive unemployment insurance fraud, with estimates ranging from $20 billion (official state figure) to $32.6 billion (cited in federal reports, congressional inquiries, and expert analyses like LexisNexis). This stemmed from relaxed verification rules to expedite payments, leading to claims paid to inmates, deceased individuals, and international criminals.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9oZWFydGxhbmRqb3VybmFsLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.MripJMFE5cJMWRrU27sxxGtVRBklUQnIxQhUKbCEy7w?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=186610473)
A [2024 state audit revealed](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e0758de5-1c35-4e4f-acec-6411ad12d9f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) California spent $24 billion on homelessness programs over five years (2018-2023), but lacked consistent tracking of outcomes or effectiveness, resulting in poor accountability despite persistent high homelessness rates. Recent federal investigations have uncovered some fraud, including cases where developers misused tens of millions in housing funds while exceeding 187,000 people in recent counts, a _rate_ of increase around 3% in 2024.

The California High-Speed Rail project, launched in 2008, has cost over $18 billion to date, with construction ongoing in the Central Valley but no operational high-speed service. Costs have ballooned far beyond original estimates, and federal funding has faced cuts, prompting debates over its viability.

In 2025, the state spent over $450 million (some reports cite up to $650 million including related costs) on a Next Generation 911 emergency system upgrade, only to scrap the regional design due to operational failures and pivot to a new statewide approach, potentially adding hundreds of millions more.

A December [2025 California State Auditor](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/043e63e2-d163-43f6-8cc8-c9bf9fb7efe5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) report designated eight state agencies (including EDD, Health Care Services, and Social Services) as "high-risk" for waste, fraud, abuse, or mismanagement--doubling from prior assessments during Governor Newsom's tenure--and flagged statewide issues like financial reporting delays and federal fund mismanagement.

1900 miles away from Sacramento, in St Paul Minnesota, it has its own blue state major scandals. Most notably the [Feeding Our Future](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/0cd784e2-53dd-424e-884a-cb8e9d0f8e07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) case, where over $250 million was fraudulently claimed from federal child nutrition programs, with 78+ defendants charged. Broader investigations into 14 Medicaid-related programs suggest potential fraud exceeding $1 billion (possibly up to $9 billion in billed amounts since 2018), involving autism services, housing stabilization, and more.

However, Minnesota lacks equivalent systemic high-risk agency designations or multi-billion single-issue wastes like California's unemployment or homelessness tracking failures.

California's issues involve larger absolute dollar amounts and broader systemic critiques (e.g., auditor's high-risk list expansion), amplified by its massive budget and population (nearly 7x Minnesota's). Both states highlight pandemic-era vulnerabilities and oversight lapses, but California's cases span diverse areas with documented billions in untracked or lost funds.

The Palisades Fire, which started on January 7, 2025, in the Santa Monica Mountains, devastated Pacific Palisades, Topanga, and parts of Malibu. It destroyed approximately 6,837 structures (mostly residential, per CAL FIRE and Wildfire Alliance reports) and 30 lives before full containment.

Estimates for rebuilding individual homes in Pacific Palisades vary widely due to the area's high-end properties, rising construction costs, and requirements for fire-hardening:

Industry analyses (e.g., Milliman) estimate an average of $758,000 to $955,000 per destroyed residential structure for basic repair/replacement (excluding code upgrades, wildfire hardening, or inflation over 3-5 years).

Real-world resident and contractor reports cite $4 million to $7 million for luxury homes, reflecting larger sizes, premium materials, and compliance with updated building codes.

General LA-area wildfire rebuild costs range from $350-$900 per square foot, leading to totals of $1-$3 million+ for typical homes; many policies (including California's FAIR Plan) cap at $3 million, leaving gaps for underinsured owners.

Rebuilding timelines often span 3-5 years or longer, delayed by permits, debris removal, insurance disputes, and labor shortages.

Broader estimates for the Palisades Fire (distinct from the concurrent Eaton Fire) contribute to combined LA wildfires totals:
Insured losses for Palisades-specific structures are in the tens of billions, driven by median pre-fire home values around $4 million.

Total economic damage and rebuilding costs across the Palisades and related fires have been projected as high as $250-$275 billion (AccuWeather, including indirect losses), with property/capital losses alone at $95-$164 billion (UCLA Anderson Forecast).

The FireAid benefit concert raised $100 million to help in the recovery from the Palisades and Eaton fires. But many have questioned where the money went and why they didn't personally receive any of it.

More focused reports suggest $35-$45 billion in insured payouts for the fires, plus public infrastructure repairs (~$350 million in LA city limits).

Many residents face challenges rebuilding due to insurance shortfalls, with some opting to sell lots (e.g., burned properties fetching $1 million+ as "blank canvases").

Recovery efforts include streamlined permitting and federal aid, but progress remains slow as of late 2025.

Top it all off with [California's "train to nowhere"](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/dcc89e5e-2c0b-4ac2-b631-b76fdca703fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (High-Speed Rail) project's cost is estimated around **$135 billion** , a massive increase from its original budget, what "was supposed to be a line from Los Angeles to San Francisco for $33 billion (already an inflated price tag) completed by 2020 has morphed into a colossal boondoggle", Sec Sean Duffy said in an [oped](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/0660af0d-dbf5-41e9-aab3-c744a4683f50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the Sacremento Bee. He went on, facing significant overruns, delays, and a funding gap for even the initial Central Valley segment (Bakersfield to Merced), leading ongoing political controversy over its viability and scale. In 2021, the Biden administration restored about $929 million in grants that the Trump administration had revoked in 2019. This year Duffy [pulled the plug](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/0660af0d-dbf5-41e9-aab3-c744a4683f50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on $4B of taxpayer's contribution of this state's mismanagement.

[Federal records show SNAP payouts in Minnesota](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/9e0e36d2-17e1-4bd1-b70e-4ff105bb55f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) increased 174% between 2020 and 2021, with payouts increasing from about $725 million in 2020 to nearly $2 billion the following year. Following [a prolonged government shutdown](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/48b9cc97-b631-4834-9f28-4b5927278492?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that saw a pause in benefits for Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) recipients, [Trump administration](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/42902884-6a8e-40c7-a584-94ae736df96b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) officials have signaled another stoppage for those who live in states who they say have faced fraud - one of which is Minnesota.

_Editorial comments expressed in this column are the sole opinion of the writer._

Total economic damage and rebuilding costs across the Palisades and related fires have been projected as high as $250-$275 billion (AccuWeather, including indirect losses), with property/capital losses alone at $95-$164 billion (UCLA Anderson Forecast). The FireAid benefit concert raised $100 million to help in the recovery from the Palisades and Eaton fires. But many have questioned where the money went and why they didn't personally receive any of it. More focused reports suggest $35-$45 billion in insured payouts for the fires, plus public infrastructure repairs (~$350 million in LA city limits). Many residents face challenges rebuilding due to insurance shortfalls, with some opting to sell lots (e.g., burned properties fetching $1 million+ as "blank canvases"). Recovery efforts include streamlined permitting and federal aid, but progress remains slow as of late 2025. Top it all off with (High-Speed Rail) project's cost is estimated around $135 billion , a massive increase from its original budget, what "was supposed to be a line from Los Angeles to San Francisco for $33 billion (already an inflated price tag) completed by 2020 has morphed into a colossal boondoggle", Sec Sean Duffy said in an in the Sacremento Bee. He went on, facing significant overruns, delays, and a funding gap for even the initial Central Valley segment (Bakersfield to Merced), leading ongoing political controversy over its viability and scale. In 2021, the Biden administration restored about $929 million in grants that the Trump administration had revoked in 2019. This year Duffy on $4B of taxpayer's contribution of this state's mismanagement. increased 174% between 2020 and 2021, with payouts increasing from about $725 million in 2020 to nearly $2 billion the following year. Following that saw a pause in benefits for Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) recipients, officials have signaled another stoppage for those who live in states who they say have faced fraud - one of which is Minnesota. Editorial comments expressed in this column are the sole opinion of the writer. This Substack is reader-supported. **I never really considered running for office. Ever. I was too focused on raising my family, and being the best football sideline reporter I could be.**
**But even in the last few weeks, Minnesota has become the embodiment of everything wrong with politics today. Tim Walz, his inner circle, and the corrupt political class are too busy:**
**
* Wasting billions of our tax dollars on fraudulent daycares
* Failing to condemn the activists storming churches
* Raising prices on hardworking families
* Sabotaging anything the President wants to do
* Forcing men into women's spaces
* Pitting Americans against each other

**
**I can 't sit on the sidelines anymore. That's why I'm throwing my hat in the ring and announcing my candidacy for U.S. Senate!

**Minnesota has long been considered simply out of reach for Republicans. The last time it voted for a GOP President was Nixon. The pundits and political experts say a Republican could never win here. **
**But this year is going to be different for three key reasons:**
**FIRST - I'm running against Tim Walz's right hand. All the rampant fraud that took place here happened under Peggy Flanagan's nose as much as it did under his. We will remind voters about that fact every single day. **
**SECOND - The President has increased his voter share each time he has run. He nearly flipped this state in the last election, and Kamala Harris' running mate was our sitting Governor! Momentum is on our side. **
**THIRD - I'm a different kind of candidate, and I know how to win. I didn't reach the top of my industry by pulling my punches. I put in the work. I never backed down. And I kept fighting, no matter what the naysayers said.**
**In my years on the sidelines, I 've seen a LOT of underdogs win football games that the "experts" said were impossible. **
**The Minnesota Senate race will be another chapter in that storied history.
[But only with your help. Will you be one of the first-ever donors to my new campaign? This is going to be one of the MOST IMPORTANT races in the entire country. clean_body: - Michele Tafoya here! I never really considered running for office. Ever. I was too focused on raising my family, and being the best football sideline reporter I could be. But even in the last few weeks, Minnesota has become the embodiment of everything wrong with politics today. Tim Walz, his inner circle, and the corrupt political class are too busy: Wasting billions of our tax dollars on fraudulent daycares Failing to condemn the activists storming churches Raising prices on hardworking families Sabotaging anything the President wants to do Forcing men into women's spaces Pitting Americans against each other I can 't sit on the sidelines anymore. That's why I'm throwing my hat in the ring and announcing my candidacy for U.S. Senate! thought long and hard about the dynamics of this race before jumping in. Minnesota has long been considered simply out of reach for Republicans. The last time it voted for a GOP President was Nixon. The pundits and political experts say a Republican could never win here. But this year is going to be different for three key reasons: FIRST - I'm running against Tim Walz's right hand. All the rampant fraud that took place here happened under Peggy Flanagan's nose as much as it did under his. We will remind voters about that fact every single day. SECOND - The President has increased his voter share each time he has run. He nearly flipped this state in the last election, and Kamala Harris' running mate was our sitting Governor! Momentum is on our side. THIRD - I'm a different kind of candidate, and I know how to win. I didn't reach the top of my industry by pulling my punches. I put in the work. I never backed down. And I kept fighting, no matter what the naysayers said. In my years on the sidelines, I 've seen a LOT of underdogs win football games that the "experts" said were impossible. The Minnesota Senate race will be another chapter in that storied history. | | ---|--- Thanks in advance for your support. I'll never forget the good folks who stepped up in the opening days of this campaign. You and I are going to do great things together. I can feel it. Be brave. Do good. - Michele Michele Tafoya Republican for Senate, Minnesota It's official - Michele Tafoya is running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota! Endorse her here:** | Paid for by Tafoya for Senate 3500 Vicksburg LN N, STE 267, Plymouth, MN 55447 |

body:
I need you with me, Friend. | | | Friend, I never planned on running for office again. But I see what’s happening in Washington – and I can’t stay on the sidelines. **I’m jumping in this fight: I’m running for Senate in 2026.** _Why?_ Because Ohioans are working harder than ever, and they’re still falling behind. _Why?_ Because __ wages are flat. _Why?_ Because costs are rising. _Why?_ Because the people in power are only looking out for their rich friends and corporate interests. No one is fighting for Ohio workers and families in the Senate. But with your support, we can change that. We can build a country where workers share in the wealth they create, where hard work pays off, and where everyone who works hard can afford a decent life. This won’t be easy – and with Ohio being one of the most competitive Senate races in the country, we know there are many fights ahead. But if we stand together, we will win. ****[**I need you with me, Friend: Will you chip in $10 split with Able Dems right now to help me flip Ohio’s Senate seat and stand up for workers across the country?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOzlTtz-Osg_YPdrY6QhRg_ItkwHBtzfcZtOCKbttRWejVBQ1prhIIM2molTvXy48LqiugoEUcI9u6V1cajb0f4di2DGzbMxgv9s1Q7BspSs535MRtnsB8kMo52czKlF25MjlqN0iP8UZuOOuoEEubDXn9F5AZ8wIlKnCcJlCwsGdvHOOYce6tC8Q6BpRxY1ME3f1nS6cnhdi1nTzs_3Ggb4IrpVbUz3-FK7mobZub4m9XcUpiVcf8GDPaYmhl5pmM_pyGP4No5yWjtGvJg-MdwZkmWpYeWd3iSpmx29W-p_c/4nt/YGFXPnG_RSOCwO9syD4utQ/h0/h001.tO0-Ij4B_gmat0ykj7NOEX_zNUHlothHs4_rKfJ6THY)**** **** | [DONATE](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOzlTtz-Osg_YPdrY6QhRg_FTNhO6Y_VfeqlRYUf_j6IILIM2Ij2UWIn5fuwLwe4IPkDYpRf9L3a3dUCpNv532GMzXmOKvo8crjEBxAhnmgzvFCba2e6zOH5Fp5yIGNvjwJFJhIlX42V-fMhGtei-IB_tWpC9DhSI3dRy8QwkN3F54hEhBMo21BGmIGlFok1EVrUZiTM1D8Rg_hWubRBoUR_eVhUEp7YHB3tBQcWsZFqN_TZb_iQmOGtI5JMl3fEQBIzHrvlwveUfEX2hS9V9EnHlEoKF2H_n8hXJiETpIbXQ/4nt/YGFXPnG_RSOCwO9syD4utQ/h1/h001.5TZsSJD1Xget3RY0aaCpaUSuoRmUY1QjU7mUIwnCxbg)

With polls this tight, getting drowned out by MAGA spending would spell out disaster for Democrats’ chances of flipping the Senate majority in 2026. So we need to respond fast, Null.

Your support will help us respond to massive MAGA spending and win this dead heat race!**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/13518?t=6&akid=2110%2E49239%2E6_uiEa)

Janet Mills

**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. Folks might've killed to have my job.
I was a sideline reporter for pro football and basketball. I covered some of the most exciting games in history. I watched sports for a living. And I loved every minute, every touchdown, and every Hail Mary.
I reached the pinnacle of my career... but there was one problem:
I had to watch what I said. I was a public-facing representative for a world-class sports network. I couldn't share everything I believed, and no one really knew I was a Conservative.
So I hung up my press badge and quit my dream job so I could speak my mind. [](https://links.trueamericanconservatives.com/s/c/74mj9A9a-hi62BTzQ98H8w6RwbAy9GSGg1jYBTVl6jkKXBMrJSfp8hf-G1HXpnkdKSOS7i2kmdQ0Rz_TBRvhmfPWOx8ImhljhlcGfndWCeNuPP0D-WvvySYKm-6BggE2eprOUGPjUmHi66eS7-e_GhAyZuPaYhYQ2zR-d7qYpYjDKJSciaursEgr9SdVtS4KOicFOUY17-8YJNDW6_F9ZZ5biZnRyDx5Fin8SSnpgGm-cm7E8U5QPdolfq1hl4tb3z9cfs_TAmuwYdA_X_cG3Dq28p_Tv08tVazOm3HzOqPDOc8JY7yUA55H87fkFezQnKoIeWQ39X9iIWZ6aA83x8IgsPV1H2XpYMqZvVpDyWBm3su3KfH8oaMelI4mfdPqlHXltQRkBoQ4xfJHorpjmT8QReOuW0QF-sj0x9njRfV3i-GFmFB9YaxeazRrbg5r1tBDNvooyHmJFhW6mYUxn6LNiJAV8YS_Y7wCW2XMmPIo054lCSwMFUccdpe0YUMy-Tb9U6zvi-Qkix4qn09HmRXC7yV8bOkJSeHoJQlwPNtY4PCLT4LT5cPxmeJjzEc05ou1598fvjJwfOhmwtblt6NSOz2l0A4xod_2tv3rp5P6DUp3o9h1goQep3wRLigAWn6fxmjB0HlW_EGLc2h7uF4G7lNY0Z1RVaiNegwkAYce1EJ62ubhLrERuOnkvdSmY4GDHaM7CFm9QLF-mIiEyPWnU80U7pkjsfEgZ4oqGh0Ws_bhUucU/_pcePZ6MP1UarsyZ4QlmYyBbn291m6GY/17)It was the hardest decision I've ever made. Army Ranger and GOP Rep. Warren Davidson **[clashed with Democrat Rep. Maxine Waters](https://substack.com/redirect/4ac3d5ba-8d82-4dd1-b70c-5989606839af?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for demonizing ICE agents and suggesting that a "Civil War" in America is imminent.
"The invasion you are fueling of our country is TREASON!" Davidson snapped at Waters. "Silence, the time is mine!"

# **Billie Eilish uses Grammy speech to attack ICE: "No one is illegal on stolen land"**
Pop star Billie Eilish made her Song of the Year acceptance speech at the Grammys Sunday night **[almost entirely about politics, wearing an"ICE Out" pin and saying "fuck ICE"](https://substack.com/redirect/84545e4b-451c-4f90-bfaf-f66c6c36c8fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** while barely thanking anyone for the award.
Watch:
[| | | | | Gunther EaglemanTM
---
@GuntherEagleman
🚨JUST IN: Americans are absolutely ROASTING Hollywood elitist Billie Eilish, calling on her to RETURN her $14M LA mansion to the Tongva tribe after her Grammys rant: "No one is illegal on stolen land... FCK ICE!"
| 12:43 PM * Feb 2, 2026 * 3.13K Views45 Replies * 50 Reposts * 141 Likes
---](https://substack.com/redirect/2eba44d3-69e4-4987-aa95-d3c7a6289553?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **What she said:** Standing next to her brother and producer Finneas, Eilish declared "We need to keep speaking up and keep protesting. Our voices really do matter and the people matter... And fuck ICE. That's all I'm going to say." Her comments received thunderous applause from the audience of Hollywood elites at the Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles.

* **Others joined in:** Eilish was one of many celebrities including Justin Bieber, Kehlani, and Bon Iver's Justin Vernon wearing "ICE out" pins in solidarity with protesters over the deaths of Renee Good and Alex Pretti in Minneapolis. Bad Bunny also said in his acceptance speech "Before I say thanks to God, I'm going to say ICE out. We're not savage, we're not animals, we're not aliens. We are humans and we are Americans."

* * *
# **" Melania" documentary defies expectations with surprise box office success**
The documentary "Melania," detailing 20 days in the first lady's life before President Trump's second term,**[earned approximately $7 million in its opening weekend](https://substack.com/redirect/b8fb9a64-49cd-4929-a318-b0950d6ee560?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** -- the best opening for a documentary in over a decade, shocking Hollywood observers who predicted it would bomb.
## Watch:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/7f3b91b1-e7fa-4e0b-991a-b4b82d2bbb6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Why it matters:** Industry insiders were skeptical based on sluggish advance ticket sales and social media posts showing nearly empty theaters, with initial projections placing the film at only $5 million. The Hollywood Reporter noted "no one saw that coming," while Rolling Stone said the film is "proving the haters and losers wrong."
* **The numbers:** Amazon MGM Studios spent $40 million producing the Brett Ratner-directed film plus another $35 million on marketing, but the studio plans to recoup costs through licensing deals and by adding it to Prime Video. The film earned a 99% audience approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes despite being widely panned by critics as "propaganda."
* **What they 're saying:** "We're very encouraged by the strong start and positive audience response, with early box office for 'Melania' exceeding our expectations," said Amazon MGM's head of domestic theatrical distribution Kevin Wilson, calling the opening "an important first step" in the film's long-term lifecycle.

* * *
# **LAPD won 't enforce Gavin Newsom's federal agent mask ban**
Los Angeles Police Chief Jim McDonnell said **[his department will not enforce California's new law](https://substack.com/redirect/dc131569-2b5d-4997-89df-dcf55459d206?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** requiring federal agents to wear visible identification, calling it "not well thought out."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/dc131569-2b5d-4997-89df-dcf55459d206?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Why it matters:** The decision creates a standoff between state law and federal operations, with McDonnell citing safety concerns about "one armed agency approaching another armed agency to create conflict over something that would be a misdemeanor at best."

* **What 's happening:** California's "No Secret Police Act," signed by Gov. Gavin Newsom and effective January 1, mandates visible identification for federal agents and permits violations to be cited as misdemeanors. The Department of Justice is challenging the law as unconstitutional, arguing it creates risks for agents including harassment.

* **The backdrop:** The controversy comes after masked mobs clashed with federal officers outside the Metropolitan Detention Center over the weekend, with protesters throwing water bottles, rocks, and debris at law enforcement. McDonnell expressed frustration with federal immigration enforcement tactics but said he'd like to see "tactical changes" rather than create conflict between armed agencies.

* * *
# **Boston Mayor Wu claims "every human being" has legal right to enter U.S.**
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu **[is facing renewed backlash](https://substack.com/redirect/5c17885d-de9c-4a59-8cd9-10d3a1a78a1b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after a March interview resurfaced in which she stated, "Every single human being has the legal right to come to the United States and seek shelter" -- a claim that contradicts federal immigration law.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5c17885d-de9c-4a59-8cd9-10d3a1a78a1b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **What the law actually says:** Federal immigration law requires hopeful immigrants to file for visas or asylum while still in their own country or outside the U.S., then go through applications and interviews before being allowed entry. Immigrants cannot simply arrive on American shores and claim legal right to enter.
* **What they 're saying:** Republican lawmakers sharply criticized Wu's comments. Utah Sen. Mike Lee wrote "Nope... That's not how any of this works... Never has been... Never will be," while Texas Rep. Brandon Gill said "Dems demand infinity immigration, then infinity welfare -- all at Americans' expense." Lee added that Wu was expressing "the quiet part out loud: The entire world has more right to America than Americans."
* **Context:** Wu previously sparked controversy in 2023 for sponsoring a "no-whites" Christmas party at city hall, for which she later apologized, claiming the invitation was only meant for "electeds of color."

name: Mess with Texas
subject: Wipe the smug smirks off the faces of Trump and Greg Abbott
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Colorado Draws the Line
body:
We are using the slogan of Texas swagger and pride “Don’t Mess with Texas” as a rallying cry: We’re definitely gonna Mess with Texas.
| | | | | [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001G2jU4N1p5f_Xps9m0ExWgDEG6AXAZpcQetof8xDmKRETGVAab6XQUa92g0g4gQBRyUCcUqAKmbVuC8w0Ir25AlQu_i_1Sn3ilyqHsm2HDnU6WpwYUa1mlMnVi0y_Ox0eVbcNLYqK-MsoaXGv7oy7N_msgunZnl3UiAkAuEiNyVDSdgX2AzPHNxbvUU1t8NOyFfUcD4g_Qum0btcrN5Ml-9JexDHNmLyz&c=_9diGMUwtcjA3J0pXAJvqxU233O0c362fC01lv84qf0UccwONGBaxg==&ch=e9815eo9S2yqDmIe-PrDKKInqEZC2k6_OcVarY6XopKnoLVsiB-xdQ==)
---
Peter,
Late last year, Donald Trump ordered Texas Gov. Greg Abbott to rig Texas' congressional districts to help Republicans build a firewall that could withstand a potential Democratic blue wave in 2026.
The result was a gerrymander so ambitious that it flipped 5 Democratic seats to the GOP with the stroke of a pen. The conservative Supreme Court eventually approved their work, solidifying MAGA's gains and encouraging further red state redistricting.
Now other red states have followed suit: North Carolina (+1 GOP), Missouri (+1), Ohio (+2), Utah (+1), and Florida (+4 GOP).
The situation is dire, but we don't have to take this lying down. We are fighting back -- and using the slogan of Texas swagger and pride "Don't Mess with Texas" as a rallying cry: We're definitely gonna Mess with Texas.
Colorado Draws the Line is fighting to stop Trump from rigging the 2026 election by leading a statewide ballot initiative to redraw Colorado's congressional districts, wiping away four GOP-held seats in Colorado and turning them blue for years to come.

We need to be honest, though: we're taking a huge risk here that could easily backfire.
This isn't a billionaire-funded initiative. This is true grassroots advocacy: the American People channeling their anger, frustration, and disappointment into action.
Peter, we're asking millions of Americans who refuse to stand idly by and watch our freedoms and democracy be trampled by jackbooted MAGA thugs to join us and fight back.

clean_body: We are using the slogan of Texas swagger and pride “Don’t Mess with Texas” as a rallying cry: We’re definitely gonna Mess with Texas. | | | | | == Peter, Late last year, Donald Trump ordered Texas Gov. Greg Abbott to rig Texas' congressional districts to help Republicans build a firewall that could withstand a potential Democratic blue wave in 2026. The result was a gerrymander so ambitious that it flipped 5 Democratic seats to the GOP with the stroke of a pen. The conservative Supreme Court eventually approved their work, solidifying MAGA's gains and encouraging further red state redistricting. Now other red states have followed suit: North Carolina (+1 GOP), Missouri (+1), Ohio (+2), Utah (+1), and Florida (+4 GOP). The situation is dire, but we don't have to take this lying down. We are fighting back -- and using the slogan of Texas swagger and pride "Don't Mess with Texas" as a rallying cry: We're definitely gonna Mess with Texas. Colorado Draws the Line is fighting to stop Trump from rigging the 2026 election by leading a statewide ballot initiative to redraw Colorado's congressional districts, wiping away four GOP-held seats in Colorado and turning them blue for years to come. | == | We need your help to wipe the stupid, smug smirks off the faces of Trump and Greg Abbott. We need to be honest, though: we're taking a huge risk here that could easily backfire. This isn't a billionaire-funded initiative. This is true grassroots advocacy: the American People channeling their anger, frustration, and disappointment into action. Peter, we're asking millions of Americans who refuse to stand idly by and watch our freedoms and democracy be trampled by jackbooted MAGA thugs to join us and fight back. == If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == We can't avoid tough fights just because we could fail. | Peter,
If you can't beat em, cheat em.
That's MAGA's strategy to never give up power and stop Americans from ever again voting in a fair election. Unless we fight back with determined fury like our lives depend on it, they will prevail.
Trump's lackeys in Congress just introduced the "Make Elections Great Again Act" (MEGA Act)--a super-sized voter suppression bill designed solely to reduce turnout of millions of voters who tend to vote for Democrats.
The MEGA voter suppression bill would eliminate Colorado's highly-successful universal vote-by-mail system and impose burdensome photo ID requirements for voters in every state.
Colorado voters have relied on mail-in voting as a default option. Mail-in voting benefits working people and rural Americans who live in remote parts of the state and can easily and securely mail their ballots with the U.S. Postal Service.

She needs to go. | | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However == you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email. | == | Peter, If you can't beat em, cheat em. That's MAGA's strategy to never give up power and stop Americans from ever again voting in a fair election. Unless we fight back with determined fury like our lives depend on it, they will prevail. Trump's lackeys in Congress just introduced the "Make Elections Great Again Act" (MEGA Act)--a super-sized voter suppression bill designed solely to reduce turnout of millions of voters who tend to vote for Democrats. The MEGA voter suppression bill would eliminate Colorado's highly-successful universal vote-by-mail system and impose burdensome photo ID requirements for voters in every state. Colorado voters have relied on mail-in voting as a default option. Mail-in voting benefits working people and rural Americans who live in remote parts of the state and can easily and securely mail their ballots with the U.S. Postal Service. | == MAGA extremist Lauren Boebert has won elections under this fair system. Yet Boebert continues to rubber stamp Trump's baseless attacks on Colorado's elections and support his corrupt effort to free convicted felon Tina Peters from state prison. Boebert has got to go--Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat who will defeat her. Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, a good year for MAGA, Lauren Boebert ran 7 points behind Donald Trump in this district while Trisha performed 6 points better than Harris. Boebert's fundraising has been anemic and Trisha has outraised her 5:1. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | Thank you, Team Trisha | == | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat running against Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th Congressional District. Team, My team has put together a**2026 Priorities Survey** to learn more about what is on our supporters’ minds this year. **What’s one thing you’re talking about at your kitchen table that you think I should hear directly?** My work has always been rooted in building a brighter future for New Mexico. And in order to do so, I need to hear directly from our grassroots supporters about what matters most to them. **[Please, take a minute or two to let me know whether your top issue is public safety, affordability, education, health care, public lands, or something else entirely — I’m listening.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124562151/598999291/1108267336?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20260202_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MzUzZDNmNS00ZDAwLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5raVMQOznwT70P3iBR0ecO5wXflc8yAM9pOFXw9T6EM=&emci=4f53d3f5-4d00-f111-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=5353d3f5-4d00-f111-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=252045)** | [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124562153/598999292/1108267336?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20260202_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MzUzZDNmNS00ZDAwLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5raVMQOznwT70P3iBR0ecO5wXflc8yAM9pOFXw9T6EM=&emci=4f53d3f5-4d00-f111-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=5353d3f5-4d00-f111-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=252045)

name: James O'Keefe
subject: He had a CIA badge in one hand and a martini in the other. Now he wants 10 million dollars.
body:
Two years ago, a CIA official sat across from our undercover journalist at a bar in Virginia. He ordered drinks. He flashed his badge. And then, unprompted,

| | [****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zn22g95qk6u9pl6o1hv2n1bag30frvindrek8rmo_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs)
[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhq0hqiui0nq71789puleq4pe3idg237fghv77n0_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs) Dear Friend,

Two years ago, a CIA official sat across from our undercover journalist at a bar in Virginia. He ordered drinks. He flashed his badge. And then, unprompted, he started talking.

He admitted the CIA withheld intelligence from the sitting President of the United States.

He described how FISA was weaponized to surveil Trump and his team.

He said all of this on camera.

We published it. America watched. And now he's suing me for **$10 million.**

**[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z92n9kh2n0bjfu9kp558qufu70oa8itv4f99nn58_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs)**Help me defend the 1[**st Amendment by donating to my legal defense fund.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zuqtk9miajs2rufv41hsih2umqujnq8ckf9i35p0_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs)****

Let me be clear about what he's claiming: that our journalist committed "fraud" by using a dating app to meet him. That's it. A dating profile. Undercover investigative journalism is the same technique used by restaurant critics, investigative reporters, and 60 Minutes for half a century.

He also claims "wiretapping"—in Virginia, a one-party consent state where our recording was completely legal.

And the damages he's demanding? Compensation for "humiliation, depression, fear, and terror" caused by... the public hearing his own words.

This lawsuit isn't really about a dating profile or hurt feelings. **It's a message to every journalist in America: expose the intelligence community, and we will bury you in legal fees until you break.**

They've done it before. They'll do it again. [**Unless we prove it doesn't work**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zdjj88dn35bif4lthqjir8qkmeq9a84p1gn0a4u8_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs).

I'm not settling. I'm not backing down. I'm taking this to discovery—where we'll subpoena documents, depose witnesses, and expose even more of what they've been hiding. If we have to, we'll take it to the Supreme Court.

[**But I can't do it alone**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zihifrt94if1n4nurr0dldos4sl5ih92mavecjm0_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs).

Legal defense against a well-funded plaintiff—with what appears to be Deep State backing—costs hundreds of thousands of dollars. Every motion, every filing, every hour of attorney time adds up fast.

**[**Will you stand in the gap with me today?**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zbtd07afaj6bs0grfa3u8upnkbj1cb5gt023s60o_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs)**

[**Your donation—whether $25 or $2,500—goes directly to legal defense**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zeqjkurnlahdt023bmbnjk8p096mlqtf46cjl6h0_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs), keeping our investigators in the field, and ensuring that the cost of telling the truth doesn't become too high to bear.

[**Your gift through Liberty Guard funds our defense so that we can keep fighting.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z92c534ef4m3uovoqj7o9uh5ig5rb8fecdsmugag_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs)

He thought the camera would stay hidden. He thought his words would never see daylight. He thought wrong.

Let's finish what we started.

In Truth,
James O'Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

**P.S.** Discovery is where the real fight begins—and where we often uncover more than the plaintiff bargained for. Your support today doesn't just defend this case. It funds the next investigation.

---

Pop Acta Media | American Action News
---
_PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zaoaeuopgpcddmn7m0me5kuommhdaebihuo7u818_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjkgqracjkmoq37d1m65qbfctjmkrncst66uqrf9bjmpqn4djm69qb4d9nkvijbo9getqb3cdkmppmeo0n6mq39cdjkorrbcp4mni6apb4s5gmeoh1k8gn48n24hjmeo30cdim8o33c8iv0)_ | _[manage data preferences](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zpusji3vc4ohiu4374240ch98mmq9lgthbrjobn8_rp22sh2s8i6epr0c1hmap30cdh2bs)_

name: Office of U.S. Senator Angela Alsobrooks
subject: Senator Alsobrooks Newsletter -- February 2, 2026
body:
|

---
Dear Friends and Neighbors, I know you've watched in horror, just as I have, the absolute lawlessness of these ICE agents who have been harassing, harming and even killing Americans. We have seen the murder of Alex Pretti, a Minneapolis ICU nurse, at the hands of these people. We have seen the murder of Renee Good, a mother. Both United States citizens. We have seen the apprehension of children, immigrants with no criminal history, and even U.S. citizens. This cannot continue and we, as a nation, will not tolerate these violations of our civil liberties. That's why on Friday, I voted NO on a package of bills that included a Continuing Resolution to add additional funding to the Department of Homeland Security. I have made it abundantly clear that we cannot send another penny to ICE without serious reforms. Masked men are killing Americans in our streets and terrorizing our communities. The legislation put forth included no reforms. So I voted NO. Be clear - each of you are making a difference. Your voices matter. You spoke out and demanded answers, and now there is a civil rights investigation into Alex Pretti's death. You spoke out against this Administration's treatment of an innocent child, and 5-year-old Liam Ramos was released from custody. There are still more children who must be released immediately. And be clear, I will be working hard for you in the Senate each day. Federal judges who know the law and revere our Constitution are making sound rulings, but your voice has the ability to effectuate change. So please - continue to speak out boldly in defense of our nation and the defenseless. And I will be there by your side. Click [here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15359.15964831.5910303.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dDTGLvzHuhEk&redir_log=093106258084298) to see my appearance on Deadline White House, and [here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15359.15964831.5910303.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dKI7ryHq4xnU&redir_log=928442555748296) to see my Morning Joe interview discussing DHS funding.
**Black History Month** This Black History Month, I am reminded of the words of Coretta Scott King who said _Freedom is never really won, you earn it and win it in every generation_. This month, we honor the Black Americans who earned freedom in generations past and laid the foundation for where we are today. Especially in these trying times, we understand that the fight continues. And just as our ancestors continue to inspire us, it's our obligation to keep making the history that inspires the next generation to secure their future. **In the Community** I was so honored to join the Women's Missionary Society of the AME Church for a powerful conversation on Black women in politics. "Power in Our Hands" reminds us that our voices, votes, and leadership shape communities. I'm grateful to WMS for celebrating faith, purpose, and impact. Progress doesn't happen in isolation. It happens together. I was honored to participate in this year's Women's Legislative Briefing Panel. Thank you to the Montgomery County Commission for Women for creating space for real conversation, empowerment, and engagement--especially in times like these. Last month I had a great time meeting community leaders, small business owners, and Mountain Marylanders at the Annual PACE breakfast. Thank you to everyone who came out to connect, share ideas, and talk about priorities for Allegany and Garrett Counties. It was an honor to join my sorors of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. for the DC Statewide Founders Day luncheon. Especially in times like these, our organization's commitment to social activism is critical. **Stay in the Fight ** I know these are difficult times. But please know that I will continue fighting for you every step of the way. We will not give up. We will not give in to this Administration, and I ask that you continue to fight alongside us. Please continue to reach out to us. You can call the office or connect with us at [alsobrooks.senate.gov](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=15359.15964831.5910303.7190525&report_id=&redirect=http%3a%2f%2falsobrooks.senate.gov%2f&redir_log=346519414955204). You can also follow us on Bluesky, Instagram, Facebook, Threads, YouTube, and X. We are in this TOGETHER. Sincerely, Angela D. Alsobrooks
United States Senator
[Click here to Unsubscribe.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/EForm.aspx?__cid=SenAlsobrooks&__fid=100022&__crop=15359.15964831.5910303.7190525)

Thank you to the Montgomery County Commission for Women for creating space for real conversation, empowerment, and engagement--especially in times like these. Last month I had a great time meeting community leaders, small business owners, and Mountain Marylanders at the Annual PACE breakfast. Thank you to everyone who came out to connect, share ideas, and talk about priorities for Allegany and Garrett Counties. It was an honor to join my sorors of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. for the DC Statewide Founders Day luncheon. Especially in times like these, our organization's commitment to social activism is critical. Stay in the Fight I know these are difficult times. But please know that I will continue fighting for you every step of the way. We will not give up. We will not give in to this Administration, and I ask that you continue to fight alongside us. Please continue to reach out to us. You can call the office or connect with us at . You can also follow us on Bluesky, Instagram, Facebook, Threads, YouTube, and X. We are in this TOGETHER. Sincerely, Angela D. Alsobrooks United States Senator

name: Jon Ossoff
subject: Broken promises
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
If you’re outraged by what’s happening to our country — and you’re ready to fight for the future of this Republic — please don’t wait until 2028
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99963?refcode=em20260202-10006&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10006%2E614464%2EahbFW6)
See, I get why some folks voted for Trump.
He promised to attack a broken system. And it’s a ripe target.
Even before Trump came on the scene, America had one of the most corrupt political systems in the Western world.
It’s been running on corporate money, secret money, billionaire money.
And it’s worse than ever now.
Citizens United was the worst court decision in modern American history.
When Members of Congress aren’t begging for money from Washington lobbyists, they’re trying to dodge getting carpet-bombed by super PACs.
Senators get threatened with millions of dollars of attack ads over the votes they take.
And see, that is why things don’t work for ordinary people.
It’s because the people’s elected representatives don’t represent the people. They represent the big donors!
That corruption is why they defunded nursing homes to cut taxes for the rich.
Corruption is why you pay a fortune for prescriptions.
Corruption is why your insurance claim keeps getting denied.
Corruption is why hedge funds get to buy up all the houses in your neighborhood, and then your corporate landlord ignores your calls during a gas leak.
Corruption is why that ambulance costs $3,000 after you just had to get your choking toddler to the hospital.
Yes, the system really is rigged.
But Trump’s not unrigging it. He’s re-rigging it for himself!
He’s a crook and a con man, and he wants to be a king.
You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you defund their hospitals and nursing homes.
You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you defund school lunch programs to help feed little boys and little girls whose hard-working parents are struggling to fill the fridge.
You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you do these things so you can cut taxes for the richest people in the country while you and your family business rake in massive sums from around the world.
Donald Trump’s second term has already done unfathomable damage to our country and the lives of millions of people.
And the people are beginning to see it.
But only by regaining Congressional power can we stop him.
Really.
The upcoming 2026 midterms are our last chance to restore checks and balances.
And my Senate race in Georgia is the single most critical contest: I’m the only Democratic Senator up for reelection in 2026 in a state Trump carried in 2024.
So if you’re outraged by what’s happening to our country — and you’re ready to fight for the future of this Republic –– please don’t wait until 2028: Split a donation of $10 between Heartland Patriots and my reelection campaign in Georgia right now.
Here’s the link to donate: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hp-ossoff-email-20260202?refcode=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99963?refcode=em20260202-10006&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=10006%2E614464%2EahbFW6)**
The average online donation to our campaign is around $30.
Please give what you can.
All that matters is that lots and lots of people are contributing what they can to power our grassroots campaign before the end of the year.
Please, contribute today.
Jon Ossoff

| [](https://www.facebook.com/lucaskuncemo/) | [](https://twitter.com/lucaskuncemo) | [](https://www.instagram.com/lucaskuncemo/)
---|---|---

Corruption is why that ambulance costs $3,000 after you just had to get your choking toddler to the hospital. Yes, the system really is rigged. But Trump’s not unrigging it. He’s re-rigging it for himself! He’s a crook and a con man, and he wants to be a king. You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you defund their hospitals and nursing homes. You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you defund school lunch programs to help feed little boys and little girls whose hard-working parents are struggling to fill the fridge. You can’t claim to be for ordinary men and women when you do these things so you can cut taxes for the richest people in the country while you and your family business rake in massive sums from around the world. Donald Trump’s second term has already done unfathomable damage to our country and the lives of millions of people. And the people are beginning to see it. But only by regaining Congressional power can we stop him. Really. The upcoming 2026 midterms are our last chance to restore checks and balances. And my Senate race in Georgia is the single most critical contest: I’m the only Democratic Senator up for reelection in 2026 in a state Trump carried in 2024. So if you’re outraged by what’s happening to our country — and you’re ready to fight for the future of this Republic –– please don’t wait until 2028: Split a donation of $10 between Heartland Patriots and my reelection campaign in Georgia right now. Here’s the link to donate: The average online donation to our campaign is around $30. Please give what you can. All that matters is that lots and lots of people are contributing what they can to power our grassroots campaign before the end of the year. Please, contribute today. Jon Ossoff | | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.

It’s no secret that 2026 is going to be a big year for our team here in Oregon.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124565133/599008788/-629675555?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDA4YjBiZWYtNTAwMC1mMTExLTgzMmYtMDAwZDNhMWYwZTRjIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=bnSm0yYTHLZbr3lwv55vYacFTlK2YVr1j1wUTUR_yxY=) I’m blown away. Because of this grassroots team, we didn’t just meet our first end-of-month goal of the year, we _exceeded_ it. Your support means the world to me, and I can’t thank you enough. It’s no secret that 2026 is going to be a big year for our team here in Oregon. It’s the first year we’re up for re-election, and it’s going to be an extremely competitive race. In 2024, we flipped this seat from red to blue by a very narrow margin. Now, I’m one of the most vulnerable House Democrats up for re-election, and Trump and the GOP think they can beat me. They’ve ALREADY started attacking me for standing up to their ridiculous policies, and as we get further into the election cycle, we could face even more lies and obstacles from the other side. We have to be prepared for the road ahead of us. This is a group of smart, battle-tested organizers, communicators, and strategists who’ve been winning tough races across the country for Democrats—and, most importantly, electing winners here in California.

So we’re asking today:

name: Representative Darin Harbick
subject: Darin Harbick - February 2026 Newsletter
body:
| | | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcontent.govdelivery.com%2Faccounts%2FORLEG%2Fbulletins%2F405dbf6/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/SbHeede_rySg1i7sU9Q73AAXzmuVT_V2wmvLTaM87_8=442)
---|---
---
Replies to this message are sent to an unmonitored mailbox.
To contact me, please click here: [Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov](mailto:Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov) |
* * *
---
Hello friends,
January moved quickly, and I wanted to share a few highlights from the work I’ve been doing—both at the Capitol and back home in the district—as we prepare for the short session in February.
**January 13–15: Legislative Days**
I spent three full legislative days at the Capitol getting a clear picture of what bills are coming down the pipeline for the February short session. A lot of listening, a lot of note-taking, and plenty of conversations about where things are headed. This is the behind-the-scenes work that sets the tone before the gavels really start swinging.
I also met with fellow legislators and lobbyists to keep momentum going on my two bills and help push them across the finish line. These early conversations matter, and January was about making sure everything is lined up before session begins.
**Going to a Town Hall to Hear Our Federal Lawmakers
(You Can’t Make This Up)
**
Kail and I attended a town hall at Thurston High School featuring Senator Jeff Merkley and Congresswoman Val Hoyle. The event was less about sharing new policy ideas and more about rallying the crowd against anything coming from the federal government that doesn’t align with their views.
That tone was captured in a moment that spoke for itself. Senator Merkley stated—without joking—that he would like to see the new Capitol ballroom destroyed with a plunger and that he wanted to be the one to push the button. It was one of those comments that leaves you pausing, looking around the room, and thinking... _Did he really just say that?_
**January 22: Public Meeting on Our Lakes**
Back home, my chief-of-staff (COS) and I, attended the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers public meeting for the Fall Creek, Dexter, Lookout Point, and Hills Creek Lakes Master Plan scoping process. I spoke directly with a fish biologist about lake drawdowns and how they affect recreation, dam operations, and the financial impact on our local businesses.
When water levels are drawn down too far during the summer months, it limits recreational use and hurts communities that rely on tourism and outdoor access. Public input is strongly encouraged, and comments are being accepted through **February 23, 2026**.
You can email comments to: **[CENWP-WVP-Master-Plan-Updates@usace.army.mil](mailto:CENWP-WVP-Master-Plan-Updates@usace.army.mil)

**
**Touring Life Flight in Cottage Grove**
My COS and I also had the opportunity to tour the Life Flight facility in Cottage Grove. This base plays a critical role in emergency response for our district, yet it is currently the only Life Flight facility in Oregon without a hangar to protect its helicopter.
Because the aircraft is stored outside, weather conditions common in the Willamette Valley—rain, ice, and prolonged moisture—cause increased wear, higher maintenance costs of approximately $200,000 more per year than other Life Flight facilities, and more frequent downtime. That downtime directly affects response times when our communities need help most. One of my Capitol project requests will be to fund a hangar so this helicopter can be protected, fully operational, and ready to respond quickly when lives are on the line.

As the short legislative session starts today, legislators are allowed to introduce two bills. Here are the two bills I am presenting that I believe will help rural Lane county and the state of Oregon:
[HB 4048](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2026R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FHB4048/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/cutPvxGLbBx6fcJfwJUH-r4g_1kAFNYWITHdKOpEKtM=442)\- Oregon’s Rural Practioner Tax Credit (RPTC) is designed to recruit and retain essential healthcare providers in rural and frontier communities. Currently, eligible providers include physicians, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, dentists, optometrists, podiatrists, and certified nurse anesthetists. One critical provider is missing: _rural pharmacists_.
Rural Pharmacists are a cornerstone of the healthcare delivery system, yet they are excluded from the RPTC—even as rural pharmacy access in Oregon reaches crisis levels.
* In 2023 alone, 36 pharmacies closed statewide, with additional closures in 2024.
* 23 of Oregon’s 36 counties have fewer than two pharmacies per 10,000 residents.
* Oregon ranks 2nd worst in the nation for pharmacy access, according to Associated Press analysis.
* Independent, community pharmacies are the most vulnerable and lack the financial buffers of large chains.

Rural pharmacists dispense and manage medications, serve large numbers of Oregon Health Plan patients, and help prevent medication errors, ER visits, and costly hospital readmissions. When prescriptions cannot be filled locally, health outcomes worsen and costs rise for the state. Adding rural pharmacists to the list of eligible providers under the Rural Practitioner Tax Credit would allow qualifying rural pharmacists to access up to $5,000 annually - providing meaningful relief that helps improve medication access, retain workers in rural communities, and increase economic stability in smaller Oregon cities
[HB 4028](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2026R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FHB4028/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/4-gzZ1yDIqe4TjiiRrl3jsJUntpNGDiDkVIDUgOco_A=442)\- Commercial insurance companies and Medicaid coordinated care organizations have a long and well documented history of restricting coverage for behavioral health services, reducing investment in care and limiting member access to affordable treatment. The effect is evident as Oregon consistently ranks among the worst in prevalence of mental illness and access to behavioral health care.
The 2026 Behavioral Health Protection Bill offers an opportunity to build on 2025’s progress by combining HB 2029 (2025) proactive audit reforms with HB 3725 (2025) strategies to strengthen behavioral health parity. By design the 2026 Behavioral Health Protection Bill does not reinvent the wheel; it uses proven concepts with no added state cost, yet delivers substantial impact for Oregon behavioral health providers and consumers. The bill is designed to reduce the power imbalances between insurers and behavioral health providers by:
* Increasing transparency from insurers by requiring them to identify the medical management practice being applied, its purpose, and whether it may trigger an audit or clawback.
* Reducing the insurer clawback window from the currently allowed 36 months to 12 months.
* Creating a timeframe of 180 days for insurers and CCOs to complete an audit of paid claims.
* Creating a timeframe of 30 days for insurers to complete pre-payment audits.
* Disallowing clawbacks from insurers based solely on "clerical errors," allowing providers to make corrections within 30 days instead of effectively punishing them for administrative mistakes.
* Allowing providers who owe insurers to pay on a repayment plan over three years, replacing current Oregon law which allows insurers to claw back payments after a mere 30 days.
* Extending Behavioral Health Parity reporting to expose manipulative tactics portrayed as medical management, such as _Coding Advisor Programs_ , that target behavioral health providers for billing common services by accusing them of “potentially erroneous billing.”

**If you would like to see the other bills I am a regular sponsor of click** [here.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fharbick%2FPages%2Fbills.aspx/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/dgUbABdqsWiouulqGobImSuHJ3Og4upykxyQN2KZnps=442)
Also, if there are any bills you would like me to support or sign on as a sponsor, please send my office an email.
##

Here are a few important news headlines that I want you to also be aware of:
**LEGISLATURE CANNOT REPEAL REFERRED TRANSPORTATION TAX ACCORDING TO 90-YEAR-OLD LEGAL OPINION**
A[ ](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl=https%253A%252F%252Fwww.wweek.com%252Fnews%252Fstate%252F2026%252F01%252F15%252Fags-opinion-raises-questions-about-transportation-tax-repeal%252F%26data=05%257C02%257CGretchen.Sims%2540oregonlegislature.gov%257C10d07ec068424496d63d08de548927fe%257C489a9c84574a48c7b72a2450511334cc%257C1%257C0%257C639041145517430138%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata=7wQeO7R98GJqhltPFbZMErgEJ4tR5wGxrvQuq6J8tQg%253D%26reserved=0/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/5FDT0S442k966haiHsP6JWHWIvk-6tFbraI_3I2-zaI=442)[1935 Oregon Attorney General opinion](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl=https%253A%252F%252Fwww.wweek.com%252Fnews%252Fstate%252F2026%252F01%252F15%252Fags-opinion-raises-questions-about-transportation-tax-repeal%252F%26data=05%257C02%257CGretchen.Sims%2540oregonlegislature.gov%257C10d07ec068424496d63d08de548927fe%257C489a9c84574a48c7b72a2450511334cc%257C1%257C0%257C639041145517430138%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata=7wQeO7R98GJqhltPFbZMErgEJ4tR5wGxrvQuq6J8tQg%253D%26reserved=0/2/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/M7dgXwgBakUB07CH6SAIgFc0s3Vq3j4NVQnSqiuiXoM=442) confirms that once Oregonians invoke their constitutional right to referendum, the Legislature has no authority to repeal the measure before voters have their say. That precedent directly contradicts Governor Tina Kotek’s recent call for lawmakers to repeal her signature transportation tax package after more than 250,000 Oregonians signed a petition demanding a vote.
---
The Oregon Constitution is clear that the power of referendum is reserved to the people, and once a measure qualifies through signature verification, the Secretary of State has a duty to place it before voters.
It is concerning that it took[ ](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl=https%253A%252F%252Fwww.wweek.com%252Fnews%252Fstate%252F2026%252F01%252F15%252Fags-opinion-raises-questions-about-transportation-tax-repeal%252F%26data=05%257C02%257CGretchen.Sims%2540oregonlegislature.gov%257C10d07ec068424496d63d08de548927fe%257C489a9c84574a48c7b72a2450511334cc%257C1%257C0%257C639041145517458842%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata=Nk954jjaZRYpFDYwGfA7baK6JmnaEDZD8TCbmQZaHrA%253D%26reserved=0/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/H983O6qgG5ey9eylmdaZ0qpG-jyNt8GOAp38EqUxT5g=442)[investigative reporting](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl=https%253A%252F%252Fwww.wweek.com%252Fnews%252Fstate%252F2026%252F01%252F15%252Fags-opinion-raises-questions-about-transportation-tax-repeal%252F%26data=05%257C02%257CGretchen.Sims%2540oregonlegislature.gov%257C10d07ec068424496d63d08de548927fe%257C489a9c84574a48c7b72a2450511334cc%257C1%257C0%257C639041145517458842%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata=Nk954jjaZRYpFDYwGfA7baK6JmnaEDZD8TCbmQZaHrA%253D%26reserved=0/2/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/KLfMpI40KTdc5Udx9CXs1C8CEk9VW1R5AwEDSLAhVTU=442) to bring attention to decisions that appear to deviate from established state policy and practice. Oregonians followed the constitutional process to request a vote, and they deserve transparency, adherence to the law, and leaders who respect the role of voters in deciding major tax policy.
**HOUSE REPUBLICANS CONDEMN DEMOCRAT LEADERS’ ANTI-ICE RESPONSE TO PORTLAND SHOOTING

**
---
On Sunday, House and Senate Republicans called out state and local leaders for politicizing an active investigation, inflaming fear, and undermining law enforcement before all the facts of Thursday's federal officer-involved shooting in Portland were released.
We now know that the individuals involved were not an innocent married couple. [According to federal authorities](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.dhs.gov%2Fnews%2F2026%2F01%2F09%2Fdhs-provides-update-us-border-patrol-portland-who-attempted-arrest-tren-de-aragua/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/CQRT1nfxjqD9I07WHLSCsI2s6gmTs_0kWUphhyM9UB8=442), they were unlawfully present in the country, connected to a violent gang, and involved in a prostitution ring operating here in Oregon.
That information was publicly available before a Thursday press conference held by the Governor, the Portland Mayor, members of the City Council, and state legislators. Yet none of those facts were shared with the public at that time.
Instead, we watched as federal law enforcement officers were publicly condemned, compared to secret police, and portrayed as threats to the community while critical facts were withheld.
That kind of rhetoric does not make communities safer, it makes them more divided, more anxious, and less trusting of the institutions meant to protect them.
As Republicans, we believe in the rule of law and accountability at every level of government. We support law enforcement at the local, state, and federal level, because the men and women who serve do dangerous work on behalf of all of us.
Violent criminal organizations do not respect city boundaries, state lines, or political narratives. When law enforcement officers act to protect life and public safety, they deserve due process, not instant condemnation.
Criminals who exploit our communities should know this: Oregon is not a safe haven for violence, trafficking, or organized crime.
And elected officials should remember this: Leadership means lowering the temperature and telling the truth, even when it’s uncomfortable and putting public safety above politics.
**NOTABLE BILLS THAT BECAME LAW IN 2026**
The previous long session was hectic. With transportation funding dominating headlines, other notable legislation slipped through the cracks. From unemployment benefits for striking workers to unfunded climate curriculum, here are a few bills that became law on Jan. 1 that will negatively impact Oregonians.
---

[**SB 916:**](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB916/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/nLnSpPEKKwTIpcMDk7nWfS9f3x77ayDPe2wO7VdZ8oQ=442) **_Unemployment Benefits for Striking Workers_ —** This bill allows striking public and private employees to collect up to 10 weeks of unemployment pay after two weeks on the picket line. Oregon is the first state in the nation to allow public employees to receive unemployment benefits while on strike. The superintendents of Oregon’s largest school districts wrote to lawmakers to oppose the bill because it could cost their districts millions of dollars. All this bill does is reward powerful unions that fund Democratic campaigns.
[**HB 3365:**](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FHB3365/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/PAeb9Yrc2qk5ARkmbQzBRoI_w0lLVYiUKMtPZTDC_a4=442)**_Unfunded Climate Education Mandates_ —** This bill directs the State Board of Education to ensure that academic content standards for core subjects include “sufficient” instruction on the causes and effects of climate change and strategies to respond to such. Climate change is already mandatory in health, science, and social studies curricula. Not only is this redundant, but it is also another unfunded mandate that school districts can not afford.
[**SB 1154:**](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB1154/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/w-dp7A1JlB6YVibpKUQTcsCVnNWrOe7cEHZ1SKbu5Ts=442)**_Ground Water Quality Management Area Regulation_ —** This bill created a tiered regulatory system to prevent and manage groundwater quality areas. Republicans were able to secure an amendment to the bill that provides legal clarity to rural communities dealing with water quality issues. However, the bill leaves the door open for future mandates on how and when Oregonians can use water for ranching and agriculture.
**FINAL LEGISLATIVE DAYS TELL US WHAT TO EXPECT FOR 2026 SHORT SESSION**
**Sales tax proposed —** The concept of a sales tax is not a foreign one to the Oregon legislature, though it is holistically unpopular. [A bill was proposed](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025I1%2FDownloads%2FCommitteeMeetingDocument%2F311175/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/K6gx_xSwyhESbHJP7DvszSwPicGph8qEt02pZ6ZqYks=442) for the short session that aims to reduce taxes imposed under various tax programs and phase in a sales tax in their stead. The concept, in theory, is not a bad one. [A sales tax](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025I1%2FDownloads%2FCommitteeMeetingDocument%2F311370/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/g8TNPkHIa41hn2-J5NYof1k9aS2vlYkryvbhLQ-cpjQ=442) would help stabilize Oregon’s revenue, but Democrats clearly see this as just another way to bloat their budget. There would need to be massive income and property tax cuts for this to even be close to a good idea for Oregon.
**Constrictive immigration policy —** On Wednesday, Democrats held a press conference where they unveiled their “Federal Response Package.” While this package touched on federal tax code disconnects and preventing the sale of public lands, the package largely revolved around immigration. Included in the package were bills to introduce training standards in workplaces relating to ICE inspections, require schools to inform parents when there has been ICE activity on campus, and prevent Oregon businesses and agencies from purchasing from organizations that are associated with deportation efforts. President Trump recently decreed that[ all sanctuary states would no longer receive federal funding](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fthehill.com%2Fhomenews%2Fadministration%2F5688157-donald-trump-sanctuary-city-funding%2F/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/gF6AfBhw30MXxVTgARoYAgzibYdyUcghzm4oEVz1VBA=442), beginning Feb. 1. The “Federal Response Package” seems to be the state’s response to such federal action. It will be interesting to watch how this plays out in the 2026 session.
---
**A backtrack on transportation to save face —** On Jan. 7, [Governor Kotek called for a complete repeal](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.statesmanjournal.com%2Fstory%2Fnews%2Fpolitics%2F2026%2F01%2F13%2Ftransportation-bill-repeal-kill-odot-accountability-measures%2F88072551007%2F/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/MCIf0MYPFBbfvRt7hdq2x4lEl5oYMrrtACxXgrA4qxw=442) of the $4.3 billion transportation tax and spend package after facing backlash and a successful ballot referral effort. Despite championing and pushing the bill through a grueling special session, Kotek would rather repeal the whole bill — including accountability and oversight provisions — than run on the same ticket as a grossly unpopular tax hike. It is more than likely that we see this on the 2026 session agenda.
---
The upcoming session is turning out to be heavy on resolutions, light on solutions. Instead of curating effective legislation, Salem politicians default to boards and task forces to address constituents’ concerns.
**GET INVOLVED**
**Register to testify!**
* If there is a bill you are particularly passionate about, you can register to testify either in support or opposition to the bill.
* Advance registration is required! Registration closes 30 minutes before the hearing is scheduled to begin.
* To register, go to [https://olis.oregonlegislature.gov/liz/2025R1](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/wLK_O6B1CVgccDHmCTrJhMAXfzGhxRUFzQ6f9jOZPw8=442), select the “Bills” icon on the top right corner of your page, enter the bill number, and select “Register to Testify.” Or, call 1-833-588-4500.
* You will want to fill out the “Public Testimony Registration Form.”
* You will receive an email confirmation with an option to join Microsoft Teams if you can not testify in person.

**Submit a Written Testimony!**
* If you prefer not to testify in person, you can also submit written testimony in support or opposition to a bill.
* To submit written testimony, follow the same steps as you would to register to testify in front of the committee, but you will select “Submit Testimony” and fill out the “Written Testimony Submission Form.”
* Written testimony must be submitted up to 48 hours after the committee meeting start time.

Testifying on a bill gives you a voice in Oregon’s legislature. The voice of the people is the cornerstone of democracy! Let yours be heard today.
As always, thank you for staying engaged, asking questions, and being part of the process. I work for you, and I appreciate hearing from you.
Warm regards,
Darin Harbick
**Capitol Phone:** 503-986-1412
**Capitol Address:** 900 Court St. NE, H-376, Salem, Oregon 97301
**Email:** [Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov](mailto:Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov)
**Website:**[https://www.oregonlegislature.gov/harbick](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fharbick/1/0101019c1f263bc4-1a14c040-583d-4a99-974f-a031d948de94-000000/H9HdzzxjvlHj0AWECACorfCbuZu5VEDHo2AykNbEKDU=442)
