clean_body: | | | ---|--- Replies to this message are sent to an unmonitored mailbox. To contact me, please click here: mailto:Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov | Hello friends, January moved quickly, and I wanted to share a few highlights from the work I’ve been doing—both at the Capitol and back home in the district—as we prepare for the short session in February. January 13–15: Legislative Days I spent three full legislative days at the Capitol getting a clear picture of what bills are coming down the pipeline for the February short session. A lot of listening, a lot of note-taking, and plenty of conversations about where things are headed. This is the behind-the-scenes work that sets the tone before the gavels really start swinging. I also met with fellow legislators and lobbyists to keep momentum going on my two bills and help push them across the finish line. These early conversations matter, and January was about making sure everything is lined up before session begins. Going to a Town Hall to Hear Our Federal Lawmakers (You Can’t Make This Up) Kail and I attended a town hall at Thurston High School featuring Senator Jeff Merkley and Congresswoman Val Hoyle. The event was less about sharing new policy ideas and more about rallying the crowd against anything coming from the federal government that doesn’t align with their views. That tone was captured in a moment that spoke for itself. Senator Merkley stated—without joking—that he would like to see the new Capitol ballroom destroyed with a plunger and that he wanted to be the one to push the button. It was one of those comments that leaves you pausing, looking around the room, and thinking... Did he really just say that? January 22: Public Meeting on Our Lakes Back home, my chief-of-staff (COS) and I, attended the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers public meeting for the Fall Creek, Dexter, Lookout Point, and Hills Creek Lakes Master Plan scoping process. I spoke directly with a fish biologist about lake drawdowns and how they affect recreation, dam operations, and the financial impact on our local businesses. When water levels are drawn down too far during the summer months, it limits recreational use and hurts communities that rely on tourism and outdoor access. Public input is strongly encouraged, and comments are being accepted through February 23, 2026. You can email comments to: mailto:CENWP-WVP-Master-Plan-Updates@usace.army.mil Touring Life Flight in Cottage Grove My COS and I also had the opportunity to tour the Life Flight facility in Cottage Grove. This base plays a critical role in emergency response for our district, yet it is currently the only Life Flight facility in Oregon without a hangar to protect its helicopter. Because the aircraft is stored outside, weather conditions common in the Willamette Valley—rain, ice, and prolonged moisture—cause increased wear, higher maintenance costs of approximately $200,000 more per year than other Life Flight facilities, and more frequent downtime. That downtime directly affects response times when our communities need help most. One of my Capitol project requests will be to fund a hangar so this helicopter can be protected, fully operational, and ready to respond quickly when lives are on the line. As the short legislative session starts today, legislators are allowed to introduce two bills. Here are the two bills I am presenting that I believe will help rural Lane county and the state of Oregon: \- Oregon’s Rural Practioner Tax Credit (RPTC) is designed to recruit and retain essential healthcare providers in rural and frontier communities. Currently, eligible providers include physicians, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, dentists, optometrists, podiatrists, and certified nurse anesthetists. One critical provider is missing: rural pharmacists. Rural Pharmacists are a cornerstone of the healthcare delivery system, yet they are excluded from the RPTC—even as rural pharmacy access in Oregon reaches crisis levels. In 2023 alone, 36 pharmacies closed statewide, with additional closures in 2024. 23 of Oregon’s 36 counties have fewer than two pharmacies per 10,000 residents. Oregon ranks 2nd worst in the nation for pharmacy access, according to Associated Press analysis. Independent, community pharmacies are the most vulnerable and lack the financial buffers of large chains. Rural pharmacists dispense and manage medications, serve large numbers of Oregon Health Plan patients, and help prevent medication errors, ER visits, and costly hospital readmissions. When prescriptions cannot be filled locally, health outcomes worsen and costs rise for the state. Adding rural pharmacists to the list of eligible providers under the Rural Practitioner Tax Credit would allow qualifying rural pharmacists to access up to $5,000 annually - providing meaningful relief that helps improve medication access, retain workers in rural communities, and increase economic stability in smaller Oregon cities \- Commercial insurance companies and Medicaid coordinated care organizations have a long and well documented history of restricting coverage for behavioral health services, reducing investment in care and limiting member access to affordable treatment. The effect is evident as Oregon consistently ranks among the worst in prevalence of mental illness and access to behavioral health care. The 2026 Behavioral Health Protection Bill offers an opportunity to build on 2025’s progress by combining HB 2029 (2025) proactive audit reforms with HB 3725 (2025) strategies to strengthen behavioral health parity. By design the 2026 Behavioral Health Protection Bill does not reinvent the wheel; it uses proven concepts with no added state cost, yet delivers substantial impact for Oregon behavioral health providers and consumers. The bill is designed to reduce the power imbalances between insurers and behavioral health providers by: Increasing transparency from insurers by requiring them to identify the medical management practice being applied, its purpose, and whether it may trigger an audit or clawback. Reducing the insurer clawback window from the currently allowed 36 months to 12 months. Creating a timeframe of 180 days for insurers and CCOs to complete an audit of paid claims. Creating a timeframe of 30 days for insurers to complete pre-payment audits. Disallowing clawbacks from insurers based solely on "clerical errors," allowing providers to make corrections within 30 days instead of effectively punishing them for administrative mistakes. Allowing providers who owe insurers to pay on a repayment plan over three years, replacing current Oregon law which allows insurers to claw back payments after a mere 30 days. Extending Behavioral Health Parity reporting to expose manipulative tactics portrayed as medical management, such as Coding Advisor Programs , that target behavioral health providers for billing common services by accusing them of “potentially erroneous billing.” If you would like to see the other bills I am a regular sponsor of click Also, if there are any bills you would like me to support or sign on as a sponsor, please send my office an email. Here are a few important news headlines that I want you to also be aware of: LEGISLATURE CANNOT REPEAL REFERRED TRANSPORTATION TAX ACCORDING TO 90-YEAR-OLD LEGAL OPINION A confirms that once Oregonians invoke their constitutional right to referendum, the Legislature has no authority to repeal the measure before voters have their say. That precedent directly contradicts Governor Tina Kotek’s recent call for lawmakers to repeal her signature transportation tax package after more than 250,000 Oregonians signed a petition demanding a vote. The Oregon Constitution is clear that the power of referendum is reserved to the people, and once a measure qualifies through signature verification, the Secretary of State has a duty to place it before voters. It is concerning that it took to bring attention to decisions that appear to deviate from established state policy and practice. Oregonians followed the constitutional process to request a vote, and they deserve transparency, adherence to the law, and leaders who respect the role of voters in deciding major tax policy. HOUSE REPUBLICANS CONDEMN DEMOCRAT LEADERS’ ANTI-ICE RESPONSE TO PORTLAND SHOOTING On Sunday, House and Senate Republicans called out state and local leaders for politicizing an active investigation, inflaming fear, and undermining law enforcement before all the facts of Thursday's federal officer-involved shooting in Portland were released. We now know that the individuals involved were not an innocent married couple. , they were unlawfully present in the country, connected to a violent gang, and involved in a prostitution ring operating here in Oregon. That information was publicly available before a Thursday press conference held by the Governor, the Portland Mayor, members of the City Council, and state legislators. Yet none of those facts were shared with the public at that time. Instead, we watched as federal law enforcement officers were publicly condemned, compared to secret police, and portrayed as threats to the community while critical facts were withheld. That kind of rhetoric does not make communities safer, it makes them more divided, more anxious, and less trusting of the institutions meant to protect them. As Republicans, we believe in the rule of law and accountability at every level of government. We support law enforcement at the local, state, and federal level, because the men and women who serve do dangerous work on behalf of all of us. Violent criminal organizations do not respect city boundaries, state lines, or political narratives. When law enforcement officers act to protect life and public safety, they deserve due process, not instant condemnation. Criminals who exploit our communities should know this: Oregon is not a safe haven for violence, trafficking, or organized crime. And elected officials should remember this: Leadership means lowering the temperature and telling the truth, even when it’s uncomfortable and putting public safety above politics. NOTABLE BILLS THAT BECAME LAW IN 2026 The previous long session was hectic. With transportation funding dominating headlines, other notable legislation slipped through the cracks. From unemployment benefits for striking workers to unfunded climate curriculum, here are a few bills that became law on Jan. 1 that will negatively impact Oregonians. Unemployment Benefits for Striking Workers — This bill allows striking public and private employees to collect up to 10 weeks of unemployment pay after two weeks on the picket line. Oregon is the first state in the nation to allow public employees to receive unemployment benefits while on strike. The superintendents of Oregon’s largest school districts wrote to lawmakers to oppose the bill because it could cost their districts millions of dollars. All this bill does is reward powerful unions that fund Democratic campaigns. Climate Education Mandates — This bill directs the State Board of Education to ensure that academic content standards for core subjects include “sufficient” instruction on the causes and effects of climate change and strategies to respond to such. Climate change is already mandatory in health, science, and social studies curricula. Not only is this redundant, but it is also another unfunded mandate that school districts can not afford. Water Quality Management Area Regulation — This bill created a tiered regulatory system to prevent and manage groundwater quality areas. Republicans were able to secure an amendment to the bill that provides legal clarity to rural communities dealing with water quality issues. However, the bill leaves the door open for future mandates on how and when Oregonians can use water for ranching and agriculture. FINAL LEGISLATIVE DAYS TELL US WHAT TO EXPECT FOR 2026 SHORT SESSION Sales tax proposed — The concept of a sales tax is not a foreign one to the Oregon legislature, though it is holistically unpopular. for the short session that aims to reduce taxes imposed under various tax programs and phase in a sales tax in their stead. The concept, in theory, is not a bad one. would help stabilize Oregon’s revenue, but Democrats clearly see this as just another way to bloat their budget. There would need to be massive income and property tax cuts for this to even be close to a good idea for Oregon. Constrictive immigration policy — On Wednesday, Democrats held a press conference where they unveiled their “Federal Response Package.” While this package touched on federal tax code disconnects and preventing the sale of public lands, the package largely revolved around immigration. Included in the package were bills to introduce training standards in workplaces relating to ICE inspections, require schools to inform parents when there has been ICE activity on campus, and prevent Oregon businesses and agencies from purchasing from organizations that are associated with deportation efforts. President Trump recently decreed that, beginning Feb. 1. The “Federal Response Package” seems to be the state’s response to such federal action. It will be interesting to watch how this plays out in the 2026 session. A backtrack on transportation to save face — On Jan. 7, of the $4.3 billion transportation tax and spend package after facing backlash and a successful ballot referral effort. Despite championing and pushing the bill through a grueling special session, Kotek would rather repeal the whole bill — including accountability and oversight provisions — than run on the same ticket as a grossly unpopular tax hike. It is more than likely that we see this on the 2026 session agenda. The upcoming session is turning out to be heavy on resolutions, light on solutions. Instead of curating effective legislation, Salem politicians default to boards and task forces to address constituents’ concerns. GET INVOLVED Register to testify! If there is a bill you are particularly passionate about, you can register to testify either in support or opposition to the bill. Advance registration is required! Registration closes 30 minutes before the hearing is scheduled to begin. To register, go to , select the “Bills” icon on the top right corner of your page, enter the bill number, and select “Register to Testify.” Or, call 1-833-588-4500. You will want to fill out the “Public Testimony Registration Form.” You will receive an email confirmation with an option to join Microsoft Teams if you can not testify in person. Submit a Written Testimony! If you prefer not to testify in person, you can also submit written testimony in support or opposition to a bill. To submit written testimony, follow the same steps as you would to register to testify in front of the committee, but you will select “Submit Testimony” and fill out the “Written Testimony Submission Form.” Written testimony must be submitted up to 48 hours after the committee meeting start time. Testifying on a bill gives you a voice in Oregon’s legislature. The voice of the people is the cornerstone of democracy! Let yours be heard today. As always, thank you for staying engaged, asking questions, and being part of the process. I work for you, and I appreciate hearing from you. Warm regards, Darin Harbick Capitol Phone: 503-986-1412 Capitol Address: 900 Court St. NE, H-376, Salem, Oregon 97301 Email: mailto:Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov Website: SUBSCRIBER SERVICES OPTIONS: • To stop receiving this newsletter, add new newsletters, ordelete your subscription services click • For questions about subscriber services, please email Information provided may not be final and is subject to change.

As a top supporter, you know I'm a fighter. I stood by President Trump when those we thought were patriots turned their backs on him. Yet, I refused to back down, and the Left will never forgive me for it.
But you've been with me every step of the way, patriot. Your support has been my shield against the RINOS and the radical Left. Now, they're coming at me with everything they've got with double the donors and double the dark money.
To be honest, tonight's critical fundraising goal is in jeopardy. I'm not one to get worried, but the numbers aren't looking good. That's why I need you more than ever, Peter. You're my lifeline.

body:
From debate stages to state tours to major endorsements, our candidates are out front. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124566604/599013389/-1894566299?refcode=WUEFEN06_20260202_StateofSenate_ALL_v1s2&sourceid=1057676&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MTU3NGMyNy01MzAwLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5pc3EVjmz9PQYF3Z_m0OdEBFYt_PeLK-9tJGJN4Ew_0=) Peter — Our U.S. Senate candidates have been busy. Across the country, Democratic pro-choice women are wasting no time showing voters what they stand for.

From debate stages to state tours to major endorsements, our candidates are out front: taking on the Trump administration, centering the issues families care most about, and building the kind of people-powered campaigns that win tough races. ********[**Before I share what is going on in Illinois, Maine, Alaska, and Texas, Peter, will you chip in to help us flip the Senate in 2026?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124566609/599013394/-1894566299?refcode=WUEFEN06_20260202_StateofSenate_ALL_v1s2&sourceid=1057676&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MTU3NGMyNy01MzAwLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5pc3EVjmz9PQYF3Z_m0OdEBFYt_PeLK-9tJGJN4Ew_0=)******** | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124566613/599013398/-1894566299?refcode=WUEFEN06_20260202_StateofSenate_ALL_v1s2&sourceid=1057676&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MTU3NGMyNy01MzAwLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5pc3EVjmz9PQYF3Z_m0OdEBFYt_PeLK-9tJGJN4Ew_0=)
---
In Illinois, **Juliana Stratton** took to the debate stage and made it crystal clear that she's prepared to take on the Trump administration. She named the stakes and showed voters she won't back down when our rights and freedoms are on the line.

In Maine, **Janet Mills** used her State of the State address to make one thing clear: she's standing up for Mainers as the Trump administration targets the state. From protecting communities to defending fundamental freedoms, she's proving that Democratic leadership means fighting for the people you serve.

In Alaska, **Mary Peltola** is doing what any great leader does — listening. She's traveling across Alaska hearing directly from voters about the challenges they're facing and how Washington is making life harder for them.

And in Texas, we JUST endorsed **Jasmine Crockett** for U.S. Senate. Jasmine is fearless and the strong voice we need in the Senate. When our communities are under attack and families are struggling to get by, its leaders like Jasmine who will fight relentlessly for working Texans.
Our current Senate slate for 2026 is full of leaders who are willing to speak plainly, listen closely, and fight for the people they represent. Your support ensures they have the resources to win tough races, and continue to lead with grit.
********[**Donate now to stand with EMILYs List women and keep this momentum going:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124566617/599013403/-1894566299?refcode=WUEFEN06_20260202_StateofSenate_ALL_v1s2&sourceid=1057676&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MTU3NGMyNy01MzAwLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5pc3EVjmz9PQYF3Z_m0OdEBFYt_PeLK-9tJGJN4Ew_0=)********

clean_body: From debate stages to state tours to major endorsements, our candidates are out front. | = Peter — Our U.S. Senate candidates have been busy. Across the country, Democratic pro-choice women are wasting no time showing voters what they stand for. From debate stages to state tours to major endorsements, our candidates are out front: taking on the Trump administration, centering the issues families care most about, and building the kind of people-powered campaigns that win tough races. ******=**** | = In Illinois, Juliana Stratton took to the debate stage and made it crystal clear that she's prepared to take on the Trump administration. She named the stakes and showed voters she won't back down when our rights and freedoms are on the line. In Maine, Janet Mills used her State of the State address to make one thing clear: she's standing up for Mainers as the Trump administration targets the state. From protecting communities to defending fundamental freedoms, she's proving that Democratic leadership means fighting for the people you serve. In Alaska, Mary Peltola is doing what any great leader does — listening. She's traveling across Alaska hearing directly from voters about the challenges they're facing and how Washington is making life harder for them. And in Texas, we JUST endorsed Jasmine Crockett for U.S. Senate. Jasmine is fearless and the strong voice we need in the Senate. When our communities are under attack and families are struggling to get by, its leaders like Jasmine who will fight relentlessly for working Texans. Our current Senate slate for 2026 is full of leaders who are willing to speak plainly, listen closely, and fight for the people they represent. Your support ensures they have the resources to win tough races, and continue to lead with grit. ****=****** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thanks for being in this fight, Ben Ray (he/him/his) Deputy Chief Campaigns Officer, Federal and Gubernatorial, EMILYs List | = | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: polling@voteractionproject.org
subject: Quick poll: USPS privatization
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
AP: FedEx board member Steiner is picked to be next USPS postmaster general amid talk of privatization [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/AP_USPS_Privatization.png]
David Steiner, a former CEO of the nation’s largest waste management company who currently serves on the FedEx board of directors, has taken over control of the U.S. Postal Service, becoming the nation’s 76th postmaster general.
Steiner’s appointment brought heightened concerns from postal unions over possible efforts to privatize the USPS, which the Trump administration has advocated for. The U.S. Postal Service is an independent agency of the federal government and one of a few government agencies explicitly authorized as a public service by the Constitution.
We want to hear from supporters in your area about this issue. Will you take our one-click poll right away?
Team: Do you want the U.S. Postal Service privatized?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=020226_2yes&amount=15] NO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=020226_2no&amount=15]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=020226_2&amount=20]

Steiner’s appointment brought heightened concerns from postal unions over possible efforts to privatize the USPS, which the Trump administration has advocated for. The U.S. Postal Service is an independent agency of the federal government and one of a few government agencies explicitly authorized as a public service by the Constitution. We want to hear from supporters in your area about this issue. Will you take our one-click poll right away? Team: Do you want the U.S. Postal Service privatized? From Notification to Regulation: How Mississippi HB 1512 Rewrites Homeschooling

_from the desk of_**Dana Criswell**
* * *
* * *
# [A Bureaucratic Takeover of Homeschooling](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1526781&post_id=185660346&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1NjYwMzQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NzAwNTA0MzYsImV4cCI6MTc3MjY0MjQzNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE1MjY3ODEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.oy5TZFM5mahRqF5aqVy7_Nou0OXIfAwmXoI9YKkp_O0)
### From Notification to Regulation: How Mississippi HB 1512 Rewrites Homeschooling
| | [Dana Criswell](https://substack.com/@danacriswell)
---
| Feb 2
---
| [](https://substack.com/@danacriswell)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1526781&post_id=185660346&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1NjYwMzQ2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3MDA1MDQzNiwiZXhwIjoxNzcyNjQyNDM2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTUyNjc4MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.xzw2xk8QtsiQ6HSoJAm4mo9azKuagVvXGSVfQNebIQg&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1526781&post_id=185660346&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1NjYwMzQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NzAwNTA0MzYsImV4cCI6MTc3MjY0MjQzNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE1MjY3ODEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.oy5TZFM5mahRqF5aqVy7_Nou0OXIfAwmXoI9YKkp_O0&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1526781&post_id=185660346&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1NjYwMzQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NzAwNTA0MzYsImV4cCI6MTc3MjY0MjQzNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE1MjY3ODEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.oy5TZFM5mahRqF5aqVy7_Nou0OXIfAwmXoI9YKkp_O0)
---
| | [](https://substack..j0JKQovw6RVsKQcuCraB__W0w38btC4fQnsT3EjNMjk?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/danacriswell/p/a-bureaucratic-takeover-of-homeschooling?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/183ccc09-1c65-408f-94e4-c1989afcee3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo by [Jessica Lewis 🦋 thepaintedsquare](https://substack.com/redirect/4ed55840-0b8a-4ac0-b6c7-13ca659b843e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/ae0a2d73-bbc4-4f5c-a534-d2d7ed7b9b91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Most parents assume that once they choose to homeschool, the state steps back and lets them get on with the job of educating their children. Mississippi law has long reflected that understanding, recognizing home instruction while explicitly keeping the state out of the homeschool classroom.
House Bill 1512 would shatter that balance.
Behind its bureaucratic language, HB 1512 does two dangerous things: it dramatically expands the state's enforcement muscle in compulsory attendance, and it pulls homeschools into the orbit of state curriculum, testing, and record‐keeping--backed by the threat of losing the right to homeschool at all.
First, the enforcement side. HB 1512 requires the Office of Compulsory School Attendance Enforcement to bring in licensed social workers and mental health professionals to help attendance officers. That may sound benign, even helpful. In practice, it means the social‐services and mental‐health apparatus becomes more deeply embedded in what used to be a simple question of "Is the child in school?"
A missed form, a misunderstanding, or a struggling family could quickly become an intrusive investigation. Truancy stops being an administrative issue and starts looking more like a quasi‐criminal case, with more officials in your living room asking more personal questions--without any rollback of existing state powers to balance this new muscle.
The homeschool provisions go even further.
Under HB 1512, homeschooling parents must submit an annual "academic file" to the Department of Education. That file must include curriculum descriptions and progress reports explicitly aligned to Mississippi's College and Career Readiness Standards. Parents must give benchmark tests in English, math, and science using state‐approved exams, under "secure conditions" with certified proctors. They must keep attendance records showing the equivalent of 180 public‐school days and submit those, too. And in grades 3, 5, 8, and 11, homeschoolers must sit for state‐approved standardized tests administered by certified teachers.
This is not a simple notification that a family is homeschooling. It is an ongoing regulatory regime that nudges, and eventually forces, homeschool programs to look and feel like public school--same goals, same pacing, same tests.
Imagine a family in rural Mississippi who chose homeschooling because their child was being bullied or because they wanted a faith‐based or classical curriculum. Today, they can tailor lessons to their child's needs, move faster in some subjects and slower in others, and choose assessments that match their values. Under HB 1512, that same family must now buy or cobble together curriculum mapped to state standards, pay for testing and certified proctors, spend hours on paperwork, and open their homeschooling to state inspection--knowing that "noncompliance" could trigger "intervention, mandatory remediation, or withdrawal of homeschool status."
That last phrase should chill every parent, whether you homeschool or not. It gives the state explicit leverage to dismantle a family's chosen educational path.
On top of the liberty concerns, this bill grows government permanently. It adds staff, mandates, and bureaucracy, with ongoing costs for taxpayers and unfunded compliance costs for families. That is mission creep, not responsible stewardship.
Mississippians should insist that education policy respect parental authority and keep government in its proper, limited role. HB 1512 does the opposite. Contact your legislators and urge them to vote no. Our children's education--and our fundamental freedom to direct it--are too important to hand over to expanding state control.
**Read all of Dana 's post and stay informed about politics in Mississippi**
[Pledge your support](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGFuYWNyaXN3ZWxsLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.gGusXcAYS7M8HWmWka33pGiP8DIlXWe7Drr8h-DaHlA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=185660346)

Under HB 1512, that same family must now buy or cobble together curriculum mapped to state standards, pay for testing and certified proctors, spend hours on paperwork, and open their homeschooling to state inspection--knowing that "noncompliance" could trigger "intervention, mandatory remediation, or withdrawal of homeschool status." That last phrase should chill every parent, whether you homeschool or not. It gives the state explicit leverage to dismantle a family's chosen educational path. On top of the liberty concerns, this bill grows government permanently. It adds staff, mandates, and bureaucracy, with ongoing costs for taxpayers and unfunded compliance costs for families. That is mission creep, not responsible stewardship. Mississippians should insist that education policy respect parental authority and keep government in its proper, limited role. HB 1512 does the opposite. Contact your legislators and urge them to vote no. Our children's education--and our fundamental freedom to direct it--are too important to hand over to expanding state control. Read all of Dana 's post and stay informed about politics in Mississippi | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Dana Criswell Mississippi ? ?

subject: Stephen Miller Must Resign and Go Back to His Evil Lair in Transylvania or Wherever
body:
He is a nightmare, possibly the worst of all Trump minions, and he needs to go back to his secret evil lair in Transylvania, where he can spend his time avoiding direct sunlight and trying on capes.
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Stephen Miller Must Go Back to His Secret Evil Lair in Transylvania](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7439a816-b44a-4762-807f-71ae8a66b885/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

He has used that power to push mass raids, escalate force, and treat human suffering as a political strategy. He promoted wildly irresponsible lies about immigrants and victims of ICE violence. And he keeps digging in, despite criticism from all directions. After years of cruelty, chaos, and division, Miller’s failures have become a threat to the nation — and his tenure must end. And if Trump won’t dump him, Congress has [**It’s time for Congress to stop this disaster. name: The Democratic Party
body:
We know it’s on your mind.
---
**2028.** We know it’s on your mind. It’s on ours too. We’ve been making news lately as our decision-making on the early states for the 2028 nominating contest ramps up. And the speculation about candidates is constant. But we know you expect more of us than to just pop up for the presidential races every four years. You also expect us to fight to win every midterm, every special election, every last seat that we can. You expect us to win back Congress this year. So that’s what we’re doing — **and we’re the only ones doing it.** The DNC is the _only_ organization working to elect Democrats across the ballot, nationwide, no matter the year. When you chip in to the DNC, you’re supporting thousands of candidates we work to elect. [**So, Derek, can we count on you to join your fellow supporters and make your first contribution of the year today to help elect Democrats in 2026, 2028, and beyond?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?refcode=em_20260202_5902_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5983_22930333_BjOT2d&akid=5983%2E22930333%2EBjOT2d) If anyone thinks that we’re an organization that only comes alive every four years, they’ve got another thing coming. But doing this work day-in and day-out requires resources day-in and day-out. [**That’s why we hope that you’ll contribute today:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?refcode=em_20260202_5902_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5983_22930333_BjOT2d&akid=5983%2E22930333%2EBjOT2d) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260202_5902_fr&refcode2=5983_22930333_BjOT2d&akid=5983%2E22930333%2EBjOT2d)**

Sherrod Brown

What if I told you that CNN and The New York Times report that Ohio is “in play” – but that corporate special interests are gearing up to spend millions against me? What if I told you that misleading attack ads have already hit the airwaves – and that whether we’re able to counter these smears and reach voters with the truth depends on people like you? name: Sign Your Name | Left Action
subject: World's Biggest Jackass Still Says He Won in 2020
body:
How many more times do we need to deal with this crap?
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Tell Trump: "Give it up, Already, You Lost in 2020"](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9e410d22-264f-4fb9-8b0f-ae36953cad2a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7517b4bf-4191-42aa-8e80-c40ba92d2914/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | _(Already took this action? Share it widely, and[click here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tef0370f5-cb29-49ea-984b-4a7dbe70d788/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) for others. And go here to see_ _[a yeti flipping Trump off](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8ea4202b-3907-4219-886c-c145312a8c7f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9))_ **[Tell Trump: “Give it up, Already, You Lost in 2020”](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tfad01d51-8cf7-4121-adc1-cb18842b8b74/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**
> _Donald, do we really need to go through this crap again and again?_ _**You lost in 2020. Biden kicked your ass. **That’s why you had to leave the White House. Remember? And yet you keep on coming up with more and more absurd conspiracy theories and outright bullshit to deny reality, and waste everyone’s time._ _You won in 2024, which is why you’re sitting in the Oval Office. Not doing your job, but instead focused on bullshit._ _**Oh, BTW, you lost in 2016 too. Hillary got way more votes than you.** But because we have this absurd system where the winner doesn't necessarily win, the loser (you) got the job instead._ _Enough of this. It’s asinine, it makes you seem even crazier and stupider than you already are, and it’s frankly boring the crap out of all of us._ _Sincerely,_ _America_
**[Tell Trump: “Give it up, Already, You Lost in 2020”](https://default.salsalabs.org/T292ccb2f-2efd-46db-87bc-a8efb3e70dcc/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**

name: Becky Edwards
subject: A Critical Moment for Utah's State School Board
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GOVERNING GROUP PAC
body:
This year, elections for the state school board need our support.

| | | | | [|
---](https://beckyforutah.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9cc818aa94e94ffd36a8547b3&id=0c595c330a&e=6484bb6dd5)
| | | | | |

Dear Friend,A few years ago, Utah made our state school board elections partisan. As a result, decisions about our classrooms are increasingly gridlocked, which harms Utah students, teachers, and families. **But there is hope this year.** In 2024, the Governing Group helped elect and re-elect 3 board members, which is significant given the board’s 15 members. This year, we have an opportunity to elect or re-elect 4 more board members. **That would mean that nearly half of the board would be Governing Group-endorsed leaders.** State Board members represent larger populations than any non-statewide office—except for US Congress**.** And yet, these campaigns largely lack the funding and attention they need to run winning races. That means modest investments can be the difference between a productive or partisan board member. [**Will you chip in today to help thoughtful State Board of Education candidates get elected?**](https://beckyforutah.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9cc818aa94e94ffd36a8547b3&id=44722505b0&e=6484bb6dd5)**** Every dollar contributed will go directly to supporting candidates committed to serious educational policy, providing opportunity for ALL students and families, and backing our incredible teachers. Whether you have a child in Utah schools or not, we all benefit from serious leaders who prioritize substance over soundbites and constructive conversation over partisan rhetoric. Even $5 will help gather signatures, buy signs, and reach voters to ensure board members are committed to real solutions for Utah education. [**Will you make a contribution?**](https://beckyforutah.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9cc818aa94e94ffd36a8547b3&id=5a5c20a4b6&e=6484bb6dd5) Warmly,Becky EdwardsFounder of Governing Group

| I never imagined I'd be running for Congress at a moment like this.
But after what we just saw in Georgia -- with federal agents seizing election materials tied to the same lies that nearly tore our country apart -- it's clear something deeper is at stake.

---
| | [](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001GoCzdx8Bf-dJTdLV44hW4sSuWNNsOzLeg5wIbJ3oVmuhfippkAzxWK5Cg2zO8xvy6FpMnfTv9y6__oPxD6MCrwXdMfXLKUlA20te6zidWRYvHh7DN9H1sSjkc1e0SapqKx0EinJy6Bt-RUopDVTIDhD2jgSsXr4jRXgISbdk_EMqXjXTfJqPPvDhF4mWfNyhfgaZ-g6CFdk39f7jr8soRu8B2lUjqcZo36DfGu35lEBWPh305trA4ByEoXyCTuw7&c=fxB3z4V2ak1kizmuZBT1HD7TvHnSbAz0IiJdD3Re2TAeCY8817YXxA==&ch=ghM0I9fI5x27eyO5VXkB7ioCHFJXqKb7oE0_Z2rV79_jnvOFbkSNrg==)
---
| | | | For years, extremists have refused to accept the truth about the 2020 election.
They spread lies. They targeted innocent election workers. They threatened families right here in Georgia.
And even after the facts were proven -- even after the courts, investigators, and juries all agreed -- the attacks never stopped.
Now they're back again. Not because there's new evidence. But because chaos is the point.
My name is Shawn Harris, and I'm running for Congress because I believe public service should be about protecting people -- not intimidating them.
---
| |
---
I've spent my career standing with working families, fighting for dignity, and teaching our children that truth still matters.
But in Washington today, too many politicians are willing to tear down our democracy just to hold onto power.
[Georgia deserves better. But this special election will be close -- so I'm asking you personally to chip in today. Your contribution will help us organize across Northwest Georgia, keep our message on the air, and show that our community will not be silenced by fear or misinformation. If you can, please donate $5, $10, or whatever you're able today. >>](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001GoCzdx8Bf-dJTdLV44hW4sSuWNNsOzLeg5wIbJ3oVmuhfippkAzxWK5Cg2zO8xvy6FpMnfTv9y6__oPxD6MCrwXdMfXLKUlA20te6zidWRYvHh7DN9H1sSjkc1e0SapqKx0EinJy6Bt-RUopDVTIDhD2jgSsXr4jRXgISbdk_EMqXjXTfJqPPvDhF4mWfNyhfgaZ-g6CFdk39f7jr8soRu8B2lUjqcZo36DfGu35lEAOt3w5zLYY-6n3yvUfY-aG&c=fxB3z4V2ak1kizmuZBT1HD7TvHnSbAz0IiJdD3Re2TAeCY8817YXxA==&ch=ghM0I9fI5x27eyO5VXkB7ioCHFJXqKb7oE0_Z2rV79_jnvOFbkSNrg==)

| | | | | | | When it comes to everyday carry (EDC), there’s no room for compromise.

You need a blade that’s quick, reliable, and built to handle whatever life throws at you. Introducing the ****[****Yellow Jacket OTF Knife****](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22385/6374453/583a2d8ea86b20623f86d284a573614df3e52695/ca1313b6d27fa12d956506b7bead2c0c26cad711)************ —the ultimate tool for anyone serious about their gear.
---
[](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22385/6374453/583a2d8ea86b20623f86d284a573614df3e52695/ca1313b6d27fa12d956506b7bead2c0c26cad711)
| | | Here’s Why You Need This Knife in Your Arsenal: ****
**> Lightning-Fast Deployment**: With the Out-The-Front (OTF) mechanism, this knife is ready to go in an instant. Perfect for when every second counts. ****
**> Superior Blade Quality**: Crafted with high-quality steel, this blade is sharp, durable, and designed to maintain its edge, making it a reliable companion in any situation. ****
**> Rugged, Tactical Design**: [Built to withstand the toughest conditions, the Yellow Jacket OTF Knife](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22385/6374453/583a2d8ea86b20623f86d284a573614df3e52695/ca1313b6d27fa12d956506b7bead2c0c26cad711) is as tough as you are. It’s not just a knife; it’s a statement. ****
**> Compact & Concealable**: Designed to be discreet yet always within reach, it’s the perfect size to fit comfortably in your pocket or clip onto your gear. ****
**Limited Stock Alert:**
These knives are in high demand, and we’ve got a limited quantity available. Don’t miss out on owning one of the most versatile and dependable OTF knives on the market. ****
**Special Offer Just for You:**
For a limited time, we’re offering an **exclusive discount** to our loyal subscribers. Don’t wait—this deal won’t last forever.******_**_******_**__**_******_**_**********
[**Claim Your Yellow Jacket OTF Knife Here!**](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22385/6374453/583a2d8ea86b20623f86d284a573614df3e52695/ca1313b6d27fa12d956506b7bead2c0c26cad711)

This is more than just a knife—it’s a piece of gear you’ll be proud to carry every day. Whether you’re in the field, on the job, or just out and about, the Yellow Jacket OTF Knife is the edge you need.

Stay sharp, stay prepared.

_~National Gun Owner_ __
P.S. **Don’t wait!** Our stock is limited, and once these knives are gone, they’re gone. [Grab yours now before they sell out!](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22385/6374453/583a2d8ea86b20623f86d284a573614df3e52695/ca1313b6d27fa12d956506b7bead2c0c26cad711)

body:
Boebert pushes the “Trump First” agenda over what is best for Colorado and America. She needs to go.
| | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However [if you'd like to unsubscribe,](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001paePEtJQus49P9sCb9BlmQyDvOi31w7iJwLVyHIDCtsxsVdoyIFBs8ENmlGMQ9gft4ssd8HtMEEYYWL9O6pQMFcapon3UpDgQJ5vwekKsMJ0DDDiPntn_2uqRHRbfoYJrldpVtoY-JDoH5UbldZkhBBHFh2DNuPwPr6g-TfexJtpBk5cvvbAAYMiaOoHflQQgAGCdj4LSk0=&c=t2_w7TyWM_Lo1v7HngB0qsHg02Ka1AVjl8-AcrkmrIRJrfTkJoNcjg==&ch=93QIlzhNqLCf1DhfcE49VljnDvnf0J-dq5FOFr1eYEne_r9DCsMxPw==) you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email.

| [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001paePEtJQus49P9sCb9BlmQyDvOi31w7iJwLVyHIDCtsxsVdoyIFBs9uJUdhupmBPAW2DLFRWzmBmssq9T6KAltfn-gDUqTFTBNfmdsPYwb_HtPzKpWEpALrodPP-TsjcD32P1j3jMH3dNNs_qT7-FxE2-rr_vqHhZfWoTtdv6biufbk141CEbr9KrmEQ7gwVXrFhPHRf0hPPT_PGjNSkvE_2V-t4nlR9&c=t2_w7TyWM_Lo1v7HngB0qsHg02Ka1AVjl8-AcrkmrIRJrfTkJoNcjg==&ch=93QIlzhNqLCf1DhfcE49VljnDvnf0J-dq5FOFr1eYEne_r9DCsMxPw==)
---
| Peter,
If you can't beat em, cheat em.
That's MAGA's strategy to never give up power and stop Americans from ever again voting in a fair election. Unless we fight back with determined fury like our lives depend on it, they will prevail.
Trump's lackeys in Congress just introduced the "Make Elections Great Again Act" (MEGA Act)--a super-sized voter suppression bill designed solely to reduce turnout of millions of voters who tend to vote for Democrats.
The MEGA voter suppression bill would eliminate Colorado's highly-successful universal vote-by-mail system and impose burdensome photo ID requirements for voters in every state.
Colorado voters have relied on mail-in voting as a default option. Mail-in voting benefits working people and rural Americans who live in remote parts of the state and can easily and securely mail their ballots with the U.S. Postal Service.
---
| [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001paePEtJQus49P9sCb9BlmQyDvOi31w7iJwLVyHIDCtsxsVdoyIFBs9uJUdhupmBPAW2DLFRWzmBmssq9T6KAltfn-gDUqTFTBNfmdsPYwb_HtPzKpWEpALrodPP-TsjcD32P1j3jMH3dNNs_qT7-FxE2-rr_vqHhZfWoTtdv6biufbk141CEbr9KrmEQ7gwVXrFhPHRf0hNfeWXIfb2dO57qnvVQRIXU&c=t2_w7TyWM_Lo1v7HngB0qsHg02Ka1AVjl8-AcrkmrIRJrfTkJoNcjg==&ch=93QIlzhNqLCf1DhfcE49VljnDvnf0J-dq5FOFr1eYEne_r9DCsMxPw==)
---
MAGA extremist Lauren Boebert has won elections under this fair system. Yet Boebert continues to rubber stamp Trump's baseless attacks on Colorado's elections and support his corrupt effort to free convicted felon Tina Peters from state prison.
Boebert has got to go--Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat who will defeat her.
Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, a good year for MAGA, Lauren Boebert ran 7 points behind Donald Trump in this district while Trisha performed 6 points better than Harris. Boebert's fundraising has been anemic and Trisha has outraised her 5:1.
[Boebert is failing. Even conservative voters don't like her. And now Trisha is poised to defeat MAGA extremist Lauren Boebert. Can you help replace Boebert with a true public servant by making a contribution of $5, $25, $50 or more to Democrat Trisha Calvarese today?](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001paePEtJQus49P9sCb9BlmQyDvOi31w7iJwLVyHIDCtsxsVdoyIFBs9uJUdhupmBPT1AADH7MAuRohMvKGOXcnphwm5OIxygtfdDRJp7jpr86lLtfsvKuXW4gFatMD927-CmmjXwNgulbgO90sg9mqJc5XXp0bOinsvVNA5pBzg_2QaPWqrrCBafVg2LIH2lljzIgDs9fIwpX8uZwoWUs746-p_D8Ad0QZDB2HIUWls8=&c=t2_w7TyWM_Lo1v7HngB0qsHg02Ka1AVjl8-AcrkmrIRJrfTkJoNcjg==&ch=93QIlzhNqLCf1DhfcE49VljnDvnf0J-dq5FOFr1eYEne_r9DCsMxPw==)

# [The Epstein Files Are a Glimpse Into America's Rot](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=186635151&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NjM1MTUxLCJpYXQiOjE3NzAwNTMxNDgsImV4cCI6MTc3MjY0NTE0OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.48k4SMjKAmMCNLSTVl1_-IlAMe_5qpCfrjsSkpEjz9o)
### Is anyone ever going to be held accountable for this?
| | [Mike Nellis](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---
| Feb 2
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=186635151&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NjM1MTUxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3MDA1MzE0OCwiZXhwIjoxNzcyNjQ1MTQ4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDI1ODExNSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.IBdEtFjwFhdt1j5dLR2kjIiOq683_LjhmhnhDtUE4Fg&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---

**[Endless Urgency is free to read--thanks to our paid subscribers. If this work has been useful to you, please help keep it available to everyone by becoming a paid subscriber. Just $8/month makes a huge difference:](https://substack.com/redirect/205b0968-7916-4772-8e83-d1a27373ed1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/205b0968-7916-4772-8e83-d1a27373ed1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
I tried to spend my weekend with my wife and son--just be present, just be a dad, a husband, watch the Grammys, and build some Legos. But every quiet moment got hijacked by the same rage: the latest drop of the Epstein files.
Because what's in those files (at least the ones we have so far) isn't just _scandalous_ --it reveals a far more insidious, enraging, and sickening pattern in our justice system. From the credible accusations against Donald Trump to the grotesque involvement of his entire administration, the Democrats sprinkled throughout, and all the rich, well-connected monsters in between--it's sick.
And I keep asking myself the same thing: How is it possible that, through multiple administrations, no one--_no one_ --has gone to prison for the trafficking, abuse, and outright violence against over a thousand children?
How the hell is that even remotely possible? And of course, we know the answer. We've always known it.
America has a ruling class right now that sits above the law and never faces real consequences. A corrupt, powerful, untouchable class of wealthy and well connected freaks who protect each other.
It's not about right versus left. It's not about ideology. And it sure as hell isn't about justice or public service.
It's about maintaining their grip on power, padding their wallets, and doing whatever the hell they want to whoever they want--without consequence. Without shame. Without fear. This rot goes deeper than any party, because the President of the United States is accused--credibly and repeatedly--of sexually abusing children.
Let that sink in. Sworn testimony. Under penalty of perjury. An eyewitness stating Donald Trump threatened a child with violence. Threatened to kill her family. As I spent time with my son this weekend, that allegation kept swirling in my head.
And what happened? Nothing. No serious DOJ probe. No meaningful FBI investigation. Zero accountability.
Just decades of silence, cover-up, and delay--while survivors are dragged back through their trauma by a system designed to exhaust them into silence.
* * *
**[Endless Urgency is free to read--thanks to our paid subscribers. If this work has been useful to you, please help keep it available to everyone by becoming a paid subscriber. Just $8/month makes a huge difference:](https://substack.com/redirect/205b0968-7916-4772-8e83-d1a27373ed1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/205b0968-7916-4772-8e83-d1a27373ed1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Meanwhile, Ghislaine Maxwell sits in a cushy, minimum-security prison that might as well be her country club, courtesy of a deal brokered by Todd Blanche--Trump's former personal lawyer and now Deputy Attorney General.
Epstein died in custody under suspicious-as-hell circumstances, conveniently ensuring the most powerful people implicated would never have to answer questions under oath. We all know that if he were alive, Trump would've pardoned him by now. And what do we get? Nothing. No reckoning. No full transparency. Just a rigged game that protects abusers and screws over survivors.
This is not just a Trump thing. He's a grotesque, bloated symptom of a much deeper disease. But goddamn if he isn't the most miserable, corrupt human being to ever sit in the White House.
And somehow, in this country, the abuse of children doesn't move the needle. Not locally. Not statewide. Not federally. How the heck is that even possible?
And then we have the audacity to talk about fixing this country--to talk about affordable housing, healthcare, dignity in retirement--as if justice is optional. As if rot at the top doesn't poison everything below it.
And that starts with winning elections, but elections without courage are just rituals. Symbolic action. Not material. It requires building a Democratic Party with a spine. What the hell happened when Democrats actually had power?
I'm proud of folks like Robert Garcia, Ro Khanna, and a few others pushing hard to get the truth out, but what the hell happened under Joe Biden's administration? They had access to the files. They had the power. And still--they didn't go after the monsters inside. Why? Because some of those monsters were close to them. Because they didn't take it seriously. And that's unacceptable.
We're never getting back on track until we tear this entire corrupt machine down, brick by brick. Until we rip power out of the hands of these corrupt billionaires and political ghouls who rob the American people, exploit the justice system, and turn politics into a game of self-preservation instead of public service.
The next Democratic administration needs to do more than talk policy. It needs to understand power--and how to use it. Because this country is diseased. And the people responsible need to go down. Not just for abusing kids, but for looting the economy, for wrecking lives, for grinding working Americans into dust while they buy their third yacht and brag about it on TV.
Most Americans are killing themselves just to get by while watching proof--over and over--that the system is rigged against them. But Jeff Bezos gets to bulldoze half of Venice for his third wedding, and Elon Musk gets to meltdown on X like he's got some kind of moral high ground--despite being in the damn Epstein files himself, begging for invites to Epstein's _wildest_ island parties. And still, we're supposed to pretend he's just some eccentric genius? Fuck that.
This is why people give up. You suffer. The victims suffer. The entire country suffers.
And the MAGA influencers? Don't even get me started. They're out here spinning new conspiracy theories every five minutes to protect their king--Trump--and every crony around him. Trump's Commerce Secretary? In the files. His pick for Fed Chair? In the files. And yet we're supposed to buy this fantasy that Trump was somehow on the outside of all this? Come on.
Then I turn on cable news and there's Todd Blanche again, spewing nonsense about transparency and how "all the files have been released." They haven't. Claiming the investigation is over. It isn't. Pretending this is all wrapped up when it clearly, _clearly_ isn't. And worse, implying the reason they're holding some names back is because the crimes detailed in those accusations--violence, murder, God knows what else--are even worse than what we've already seen. Which should terrify everyone.
If you've seen even a fraction of those documents, you know how bad it is. And the fact that survivors have to keep reliving that trauma in a system that doesn't give a shit? That's the real indictment of where we are. That's the rot at the center of the American experience: the average person is suffering, giving up on democracy, on justice, on community, because they can see the system is rigged against them. When monsters like Trump and Musk and the rest can walk free, why would anyone feel hope? Why would anyone bother to participate? Why would you work harder, vote harder, believe harder, when everything is stacked against you?
Most Americans are killing themselves just to get by, while these billionaires flaunt their wealth, abuse kids, and get away with it. And the people who are supposed to protect us--our leaders, our law enforcement, our institutions--either can't or won't do shit. They lie. They cover. They protect their own. And they laugh at the rest of us like it's all some game.
But politics shouldn't be a game. And justice shouldn't be selective. Government shouldn't be about protecting predators and crooks. It should be about helping regular people get a damn job, earn a decent wage, raise their family, retire with some dignity. That's what this whole thing is supposed to be about. But it hasn't been for a long time. And I am so tired.
I'm tired and I'm furious. Because all I wanted this weekend was to be a husband. A dad. A guy on the couch watching the Grammys. But I couldn't stop thinking about this horror show we're all living in. I'm exhausted by what this country has become.
And let me leave you with this: it's not just Trump. He's the rotting face on the front of it, but the disease runs deeper. And every one of us has to find a way to push back. To call it out. To demand better. To be useful, however we can. Big ways. Small ways. Just find yours.
Anger is not the problem. Apathy is. Stay angry. Just aim it well.

clean_body: Endless Urgency is free to read--thanks to our paid subscribers. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Is anyone ever going to be held accountable for this? | | | Feb 2 | | | | | | | | | | | | I tried to spend my weekend with my wife and son--just be present, just be a dad, a husband, watch the Grammys, and build some Legos. But every quiet moment got hijacked by the same rage: the latest drop of the Epstein files. Because what's in those files (at least the ones we have so far) isn't just scandalous --it reveals a far more insidious, enraging, and sickening pattern in our justice system. From the credible accusations against Donald Trump to the grotesque involvement of his entire administration, the Democrats sprinkled throughout, and all the rich, well-connected monsters in between--it's sick. And I keep asking myself the same thing: How is it possible that, through multiple administrations, no one--no one --has gone to prison for the trafficking, abuse, and outright violence against over a thousand children? How the hell is that even remotely possible? And of course, we know the answer. We've always known it. America has a ruling class right now that sits above the law and never faces real consequences. A corrupt, powerful, untouchable class of wealthy and well connected freaks who protect each other. It's not about right versus left. It's not about ideology. And it sure as hell isn't about justice or public service. It's about maintaining their grip on power, padding their wallets, and doing whatever the hell they want to whoever they want--without consequence. Without shame. Without fear. This rot goes deeper than any party, because the President of the United States is accused--credibly and repeatedly--of sexually abusing children. Let that sink in. Sworn testimony. Under penalty of perjury. An eyewitness stating Donald Trump threatened a child with violence. Threatened to kill her family. As I spent time with my son this weekend, that allegation kept swirling in my head. And what happened? Nothing. No serious DOJ probe. No meaningful FBI investigation. Zero accountability. Just decades of silence, cover-up, and delay--while survivors are dragged back through their trauma by a system designed to exhaust them into silence. * Meanwhile, Ghislaine Maxwell sits in a cushy, minimum-security prison that might as well be her country club, courtesy of a deal brokered by Todd Blanche--Trump's former personal lawyer and now Deputy Attorney General. Epstein died in custody under suspicious-as-hell circumstances, conveniently ensuring the most powerful people implicated would never have to answer questions under oath. We all know that if he were alive, Trump would've pardoned him by now. And what do we get? Nothing. No reckoning. No full transparency. Just a rigged game that protects abusers and screws over survivors. This is not just a Trump thing. He's a grotesque, bloated symptom of a much deeper disease. But goddamn if he isn't the most miserable, corrupt human being to ever sit in the White House. And somehow, in this country, the abuse of children doesn't move the needle. Not locally. Not statewide. Not federally. How the heck is that even possible? And then we have the audacity to talk about fixing this country--to talk about affordable housing, healthcare, dignity in retirement--as if justice is optional. As if rot at the top doesn't poison everything below it. And that starts with winning elections, but elections without courage are just rituals. Symbolic action. Not material. It requires building a Democratic Party with a spine. What the hell happened when Democrats actually had power? I'm proud of folks like Robert Garcia, Ro Khanna, and a few others pushing hard to get the truth out, but what the hell happened under Joe Biden's administration? They had access to the files. They had the power. And still--they didn't go after the monsters inside. Why? Because some of those monsters were close to them. Because they didn't take it seriously. And that's unacceptable. We're never getting back on track until we tear this entire corrupt machine down, brick by brick. Until we rip power out of the hands of these corrupt billionaires and political ghouls who rob the American people, exploit the justice system, and turn politics into a game of self-preservation instead of public service. The next Democratic administration needs to do more than talk policy. It needs to understand power--and how to use it. Because this country is diseased. And the people responsible need to go down. Not just for abusing kids, but for looting the economy, for wrecking lives, for grinding working Americans into dust while they buy their third yacht and brag about it on TV. Most Americans are killing themselves just to get by while watching proof--over and over--that the system is rigged against them. But Jeff Bezos gets to bulldoze half of Venice for his third wedding, and Elon Musk gets to meltdown on X like he's got some kind of moral high ground--despite being in the damn Epstein files himself, begging for invites to Epstein's wildest island parties. And still, we're supposed to pretend he's just some eccentric genius? Fuck that. This is why people give up. You suffer. The victims suffer. The entire country suffers. And the MAGA influencers? Don't even get me started. They're out here spinning new conspiracy theories every five minutes to protect their king--Trump--and every crony around him. Trump's Commerce Secretary? In the files. His pick for Fed Chair? In the files. And yet we're supposed to buy this fantasy that Trump was somehow on the outside of all this? Come on. Then I turn on cable news and there's Todd Blanche again, spewing nonsense about transparency and how "all the files have been released." They haven't. Claiming the investigation is over. It isn't. Pretending this is all wrapped up when it clearly, clearly isn't. And worse, implying the reason they're holding some names back is because the crimes detailed in those accusations--violence, murder, God knows what else--are even worse than what we've already seen. Which should terrify everyone. If you've seen even a fraction of those documents, you know how bad it is. And the fact that survivors have to keep reliving that trauma in a system that doesn't give a shit? That's the real indictment of where we are. That's the rot at the center of the American experience: the average person is suffering, giving up on democracy, on justice, on community, because they can see the system is rigged against them. When monsters like Trump and Musk and the rest can walk free, why would anyone feel hope? Why would anyone bother to participate? Why would you work harder, vote harder, believe harder, when everything is stacked against you? Most Americans are killing themselves just to get by, while these billionaires flaunt their wealth, abuse kids, and get away with it. And the people who are supposed to protect us--our leaders, our law enforcement, our institutions--either can't or won't do shit. They lie. They cover. They protect their own. And they laugh at the rest of us like it's all some game. But politics shouldn't be a game. And justice shouldn't be selective. Government shouldn't be about protecting predators and crooks. It should be about helping regular people get a damn job, earn a decent wage, raise their family, retire with some dignity. That's what this whole thing is supposed to be about. But it hasn't been for a long time. And I am so tired. I'm tired and I'm furious. Because all I wanted this weekend was to be a husband. A dad. A guy on the couch watching the Grammys. But I couldn't stop thinking about this horror show we're all living in. I'm exhausted by what this country has become. And let me leave you with this: it's not just Trump. He's the rotting face on the front of it, but the disease runs deeper. And every one of us has to find a way to push back. To call it out. To demand better. To be useful, however we can. Big ways. Small ways. Just find yours. Anger is not the problem. Apathy is. Stay angry. Just aim it well. | Peter,

I wanted to start February by saying thank you.

Because of your support we closed out January with real momentum. How we start the year shapes everything that follows and you helped put this campaign in a strong position early.

Everyday election day is inching closer and closer and the pressure on this seat is rising.

If Democrats are going to take back the House this year the path runs straight through this district. I’m ready for the work ahead and I’m grateful to have you in this with me. But we can’t slow down now.

name: Christina Bohannan
subject: What a powerful way to start 2026
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124571034/599029708/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTljMjZmMS01YzAwLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TjFl7QhngEy4g3Sxop7R1McZuM9XoiIhI8NREfKhTwk=)

Peter – When my finance team called me this morning, I was truly elated.

Not only did we reach our end-of-month goal, we exceeded it! And it's thanks to this incredible team.

Look, asking for your hard-earned dollars is not my favorite thing to do. I love going out and talking to folks across the district, learning about your needs and explaining how my goals and priorities are aligned with each of our concerns. But unfortunately, that's not the only way we can win this race.

Mariannette Miller-Meeks has corporate donors at her disposal, ready to throw money into this race to ensure she keeps this seat in Washington, D.C.'s hands. Every dollar we raise, every grassroots supporter we earn, helps us stand up to the political machine and spread our message far and wide.

From now until Election Day, every month is going to get tougher. Goals will be higher, the need for support will be greater, but I have no doubt that this team can deliver. **Because there is no greater motivation than bringing positive change to our district.**

**[February is a short month, and we need to make every single day count. Will you help me get ahead on our February fundraising with a $5 donation today?>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124571036/599029709/-2011951990?refcode=em26_260202_EOMTY_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_campaign=em26_260202_EOMTY_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=299c26f1-5c00-f111-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTljMjZmMS01YzAwLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TjFl7QhngEy4g3Sxop7R1McZuM9XoiIhI8NREfKhTwk=)**

I'm so grateful for all your support,

Christina

Christina Bohannan is a mom, law professor, engineer and former state representative. She's running for Congress to defeat Mariannette Miller-Meeks and flip IA-01 blue.

clean_body: | = Peter – When my finance team called me this morning, I was truly elated. Not only did we reach our end-of-month goal, we exceeded it! And it's thanks to this incredible team. Look, asking for your hard-earned dollars is not my favorite thing to do. I love going out and talking to folks across the district, learning about your needs and explaining how my goals and priorities are aligned with each of our concerns. But unfortunately, that's not the only way we can win this race. Mariannette Miller-Meeks has corporate donors at her disposal, ready to throw money into this race to ensure she keeps this seat in Washington, D.C.'s hands. Every dollar we raise, every grassroots supporter we earn, helps us stand up to the political machine and spread our message far and wide. From now until Election Day, every month is going to get tougher. Goals will be higher, the need for support will be greater, but I have no doubt that this team can deliver. Because there is no greater motivation than bringing positive change to our district. = I'm so grateful for all your support, Christina Christina Bohannan is a mom, law professor, engineer and former state representative. She's running for Congress to defeat Mariannette Miller-Meeks and flip IA-01 blue. name: Tim Walz
subject: This is unamerican
body:
[1]LeftNet

I wanted to emphasize just how serious of a moment we are in.

Donald Trump’s federal agents have been going door to door in Minnesota
looking for folks based on their accent or color of their skin. Kids are
afraid to go to school, small businesses are hurting, another life has
been lost, and the people responsible have yet to be held accountable.
Instead, Donald Trump’s DOJ is investigating me.

Why? Because my focus has always been protecting the people of Minnesota.

They’re trying to scare me and our state — but Minnesota will not be
intimidated into silence and neither will I. This legal battle will be
expensive, that’s why they’re doing this. They want me to back down and
surrender, but I won’t.

[ https://act.leftnet.org/go/21583?c0=partner_ms_tw_em_20260202_leftnet.xiEtsc&refcode=em20260202-1496&t=1001&akid=1496%2E2118727%2EytMx36 ]I need your help in this fight. If you’re able, will you make a
donation of any amount to our Legal Defense fund to make sure we can
continue to stand up to Donald Trump and his DOJ?

[ https://act.leftnet.org/go/21583?c0=partner_ms_tw_em_20260202_leftnet.xiEtsc&refcode=em20260202-1496&t=1002&akid=1496%2E2118727%2EytMx36 ]DONATE ››

I’m grateful,

Tim Walz

[ https://act.leftnet.org/go/21583?c0=partner_ms_tw_em_20260202_leftnet.xiEtsc&refcode=em20260202-1496&t=1003&akid=1496%2E2118727%2EytMx36 ][IMG]

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.leftnet.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1496%2E2118727%2EytMx36

As we head into 2026, Heritage Action is at a crossroads.

The Conservative movement has momentum — from historic policy wins to growing grassroots engagement across the country. **But Radical Democrats are already mobilizing for the midterm elections, determined to block our progress.**

**That's why we need to hear from you: What should Heritage Action's priorities be for 2026?**

name: Ilhan Omar
subject: Donate to a charity on the ground in Minnesota
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=2066%2E791427%2E0IS5VG)
Friend, I appreciate everyone who has donated to support the Minneapolis community as we face down ICE’s occupation.
With your help, we have raised tens of thousands of dollars for important charities and organizations on the ground. **Let’s keep it going.**
Today, **[I’m hoping you will consider donating to UNITE HERE Local #17’s hardship fund,](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4878?t=2&akid=2066%2E791427%2E0IS5VG)** which provides its members with food and mutual aid.
**[CHIP IN NOW](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4878?t=3&akid=2066%2E791427%2E0IS5VG)**
UNITE HERE Local 17 is a union that represents thousands of hospitality workers throughout Minnesota.
Many of their members are immigrants and people of color. Many are fearful of going to work or leaving their home at all as ICE terrorizes the community and tears apart families. It’s left folks in a precarious position, with the cost of living continuing to rise.
UNITE HERE’s hardship fund is currently supporting these families and helping them make ends meet. But they need as many donations as they can get right now to keep up with the quickly growing need for help.
So please: **[Make a donation to UNITE HERE’s Local #17’s hardship fund and support working class families in Minnesota.](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4878?t=4&akid=2066%2E791427%2E0IS5VG)**
**[DONATE NOW](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4878?t=5&akid=2066%2E791427%2E0IS5VG)**
Thank you for lending a helping hand to our community today.
In solidarity,
Ilhan Omar

name: Ro Khanna
subject: I’m standing up to ICE today—I need your help
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Peter,
FIRST: Senator Bernie Sanders introduced an amendment to repeal an additional $75 billion in funding for ICE , a massive increase that would triple their budget.
Every single Democrat voted yes on that amendment.
NOW: That repeal was stripped from the final funding package, and Democrats are being asked to vote to continue funding ICE anyway.
I won’t do it.
I’m not just a no. I’m a firm NO.
I am organizing an effort for all my Democratic colleagues to vote no.
Democrats cannot vote to continue funding ICE when they are killing American citizens and when there is no provision to undo the tripling of their budget.
That’s why I said this on Meet the Press this weekend:
https://x.com/RoKhanna/status/2017975444557975799 [https://x.com/RoKhanna/status/2017975444557975799]
Standing up like this comes with a cost. The pressure is intense. The attacks are already starting.
Will you have my back? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-fund?refcode=20260202-ice-funding]
I don’t take PAC money. I don’t take lobbyist money. I don’t take corporate money. The only reason I’m able to stand up to entrenched special interests is because I’m backed by tens of thousands of people like you.
👉 Can you chip in $3, $10, or whatever you can to help me keep fighting and push a new moral vision for our nation? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-fund?refcode=20260202-ice-funding]

# Magsig Reports Over $800,000 Raised - $600,000 Cash on Hand for Senate Run
---
|
---
| | **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE** February 2nd, 2026Contact: nathan@nathanmagsig.com
---
| | | | | | **(Clovis, CA)** \- State Senate District 12 candidate Nathan Magsig announced today that his campaign continues to build strong momentum, reporting more than **$800,000 raised** and **over $600,000 cash on hand** in his most recent campaign finance filing.
_“This level of support shows that our message of common-sense leadership, public safety, and fiscal responsibility is resonating with voters throughout the district,”_ said Magsig. _“I’m grateful for the confidence so many individuals, families, and community leaders have placed in our campaign.”_
The strong fundraising report positions the Magsig campaign with the resources needed to communicate directly with voters across Senate District 12, which spans Fresno, Kern, and Tulare counties. With significant cash on hand, the campaign is well-prepared to continue outreach efforts through voter contact, grassroots organizing, and paid communications.
Magsig is currently the only candidate in the race for State Senate District 12.
---
| | ###
Nathan Magsig is a current Fresno County Supervisor, former Mayor of Clovis, and is running for the 12th Senate District. His family, wife Julie and their three children, reside in Clovis, CA.
